items.entry: A set of gatherer's supplies, a set of common clothes, a strangely-shaped object 3 inches long that you found while gathering and have yet to identify, and a belt pouch containing lichens, barks, berries, rare stones, and mushrooms worth up to 10 ps in the Great Valley.

entries.name: Feature: Sharp-Eyed Forager
entries: When foraging for food in the wilderness, you gather double the normal amount, thanks to your knowledge of nature's bounty. In addition, you excel at picking out small details in a crowded environment, such as a forest or marketplace, as long as you know what you're looking for. If you are familiar with an object or have had it described in detail, you can notice its presence if you see it.

entries.name: Suggested Characteristics
entries: Gatherers play a vital, but little-respected role in clan life. They generally occupy a low-status position, and are thought of as the common folk, less heroic than hunters and less mystical than shamans. This everyday role shapes many gatherers into quiet observers, who have their own ways of moving through the world, and are used to solitude, peace and quiet, and following their instincts to discover that which interests them.

entries.caption: Personality Trait
rows: I am a collector, always tossing interesting objects into my bag.
I'm constantly on the lookout for things I know my companions like.
I rarely stay in the same place for long.
I can identify every bird and insect by its song.
I'm unused to people being interested in me or my story, and assume I'm a nobody.
I take long walks before my companions wake up.
I keep meticulous order in my things, making sure that I know exactly what and where everything is.
I collect stories as well as food, and always ask for more details whenever I hear a tale.

entries.caption: Ideal
rows: Provision. I live to ensure others don't starve. (Good)
Patience. Everything worth doing takes time. (Any)
Curiosity. I wonder what's under every stone. (Chaotic)
Duty. We must all do what is required of us. (Lawful)
Plunder. I take what I want. (Evil)
Change. Nothing is constant, so I must change with the world. (Chaotic)

entries.caption: Bond
rows: I cannot leave an area unexplored.
I once stumbled across a hag's hut in the wilderness. What happened inside changed me forever.
I found a lost artifact in the wilderness and seek to restore it to its proper place.
On a gathering walk, I stumbled through Nod and lost my clan. I'm still searching for them.
While I was out gathering, my clan was slain by raiders. I seek to avenge them.
I see all of life as a kind of gathering walk, and my goal is to be the greatest gatherer who ever collected.

entries.caption: Flaw
rows: The line between innocent gathering and theft doesn't exist for me.
I'm far too curious for my own good.
I have a tendency to wander off if I get bored.
I trust only my own judgment, even on topics about which I know very little.
My first instinct in combat is to run and hide.
I make sure new foods are safe by slipping them to others without their knowledge.

items.name: Skill Proficiencies:
items.entry: {@skill Athletics}, {@skill Survival}

items.name: Tool Proficiencies:
items.entry: Butcher's tools, one type of gaming set

items.name: Equipment:
items.entry: A set of butcher's tools, a set of common clothes, a trophy from a hunt you'll never forget, and a belt pouch containing pelts, ivory, antlers, and claws worth up to 10 ps in the Great Valley.

entries.name: Feature: Hunting Tales
entries: You have thrilling stories of your times on the hunting trail and can entertain a gathered crowd by recounting your adventures in the wilderness. You are at ease with other hunters, and can find common ground swapping tales of the hunt. Using these contacts, you can learn general information about an area—common prey and predators, notable geographical features or environmental hazards, and a general sense of weather patterns and animal movements.

entries.name: Suggested Characteristics
entries: While loitering around the encampment, hunters are generally seen as lazy braggarts and gamblers. But once the call goes out for nearby quarry, they spring into action with focus and ferocity. Hunters are a culture of their own, jockeying for position and status on the hunt, seeking the killing blow, eager to win glory and provide for their people.

entries.caption: Personality Trait
rows: I look after others, knowing a hunting party is only as strong as its weakest member.
I'm always on the watch for signs of quarry nearby.
As long as I'm interested in something, my patience and endurance is endless.
My respect is hard to earn, but I make a fierce ally.
I run ahead and expect others to keep up.
I let others do the thinking, planning, and worrying—I'm simply a living weapon.
I enjoy the hunt only as a means to an end, such as a good meal or the praise of others.
Whenever I have a spare moment I check and maintain my weapons and tools.

entries.caption: Ideal
rows: Honor. I respect traditions and carry out my life according to a certain code. (Lawful)
Excitement. I love the thrill of the hunt, where anything can happen. (Chaotic)
Violence. The hunt satisfies my craving for pain and bloodshed. (Evil)
Unity. I am one with those who hunt with me. (Lawful)
Courage. I defy fear to do what must be done. (Good)
Praise. I live for the admiration of others. (Neutral)

entries.caption: Bond
rows: My companion was slain by a terrible monster. I still hunt it to this day.
I will embark on any hunt that promises glory and a good fight.
I feel a strong pull towards violence, and must hunt or else commit much more terrible acts.
All I really want is a child to whom I can pass on the tradition of the hunt.
I live to hunt down a fantastic creature that the elders of my clan insist is only a legend.
It is my duty to hunt to provide for those I care about.

entries.caption: Flaw
rows: I expect others to serve me and defer to me.
My solution to every problem is violence.
I don't care about doing the right thing unless there's someone there to admire it.
I live by an excessively strict code and refuse to deviate on even the smallest thing.
I judge others quickly based on their appearance and accomplishments.
I assume everyone admires me and wants to hear endless stories of my hunting prowess.

entries: I once pursued a fiendish visitant through dreams and nightmares for seven months until I cornered it underneath the six cascades of Seerfall. As it backed into the glowing circle of its summoned evil, I realized that I was the one who had been hunted. (But try telling that story to a band of hunters who just want to compare scars.)

items.name: Skill Proficiencies:
items.entry: {@skill Animal Handling}, {@skill Intimidation}

items.name: Tool Proficiencies:
items.entry: Butcher's tools, vehicles (land)

items.name: Equipment:
items.entry: A set of butcher's tools, a set of common clothes, a keepsake from the beasts you tend (such as a bundle of feathers, a broken tusk or horn, or a satchel of scales or quills), and a belt pouch containing food for beasts, wool, a chewed toy, and salt crystals worth up to 10 ps in the Great Valley.

entries.name: Feature: Good With Animals
entries: If you approach a beast of a type with which you are familiar in a non-aggressive fashion, its instinct is to trust you, even if it doesn't trust others of your kind. The beast may or may not listen to that instinct, depending on its experience and other forces at work. Beasts that know you like you and seek to please and obey you to the best of their ability, but will not fight for you unless you have formed a special bond with them.

entries.name: Suggested Characteristics
entries: It takes a certain kind of soul to spend their days in the company of wild beasts—for even the best-bred creatures in Planegea are only a generation or two from feral. Most keepers of beasts are empathetic, observant, and at least a little off-putting to other mortals in their manner of talk.

entries.caption: Personality Trait
rows: My first impression of people is typically their smell.
I understand other mortals by comparing their actions to the behavior of beasts.
If I don't respect someone, I simply ignore them.
There's always an animal within arm's reach of me.
I always talk to beasts before I talk to people.
I'm gruff and aloof with those who are ignorant or afraid of animals.
I always save a bit of my food to train a beast later, even when there's not enough to go around.
I have a boundless affection for all living things, whether I should or not.

entries.caption: Ideal
rows: Nature. The natural order is better than the laws of mortals. (Neutral)
Respect. I treat beasts with respect, and they repay me in kind. (Good)
Dominance. I like beasts because my will is stronger than theirs. (Evil)
Service. We all serve others—as the beasts serve us, so we serve the gods. (Lawful)
Adaptation. Beasts change to stay alive, and so do I. (Chaotic)
Balance. Birth and death, summer and winter… it's all a cycle that must be kept in balance. (Neutral)

entries.caption: Bond
rows: I'm bonded to a loyal beast that I raised from birth.
Cruelty to animals is an offense punishable by death.
I'm caring for a large egg, but I don't know what will hatch from it.
I care for beasts because I was raised by beasts.
I grew up in the Dire Grazelands and have spent more time on beastback than on the ground.
A beast I cared for from youth went berserk and trampled my family. I seek to find it and cure it—or kill it.

entries.caption: Flaw
rows: I oversimplify everything to the most basic terms.
I treat people like animals, using short commands and condescending tones.
I'm as awkward with people as I am good with beasts.
I feed and nurture my grudges just like my animals.
I treat even the most dangerous monsters as intriguing potential pets.
I reek of beasts, but am completely oblivious to it.

items.name: Skill Proficiencies:
items.entry: {@skill Perception}, {@skill Performance}

items.name: Tool Proficiencies:
items.entry: Artist's supplies, weaver's tools

items.name: Equipment:
items.entry: A set of artist's supplies, a set of colorful common clothes, a collection of dyes and brightly colored powders, and a belt pouch containing carved trinkets and a saurian totem worth up to 15 ps in the Great Valley.

entries.name: Feature: Artist's Eye
entries: You know how to appreciate and understand artwork, having spent time surrounded by artists of all kinds in your clan. You grasp the subtleties of aesthetic work, and can discuss its details with an artisan, even if you're not skilled in the production of the work. Artists and artisans see you as a person of taste and are inclined to regard you as friendly and trustworthy. They are willing to lend you supplies and offer advice and information as you request.

entries.name: Suggested Characteristics
entries: Lion Clan members are seen as aloof artists, who endure all pain and suffering without any reaction but to create. They are believed by most to be a little eerie—rumors say they never sleep in tents, even in the rain or wind, lying on the hard ground instead. They are also said to be unfeeling, and a little cruel, having no sympathy for the suffering of others.

entries.caption: Personality Trait
rows: I never complain. Never.
My way of processing fear is by sculpting it. I have a collection of truly terrifying carved figurines.
I refuse to take shelter or wear any but the lightest clothing, even in extreme weather.
There's nothing I like better than going past my comfort zone—and pulling my companions along with me.
I tend to speak in metaphors.
No matter how bad things are, I always expect them to get worse.
I scare and prank my companions to toughen them up.
I focus on an object for hours without losing interest.

entries.caption: Ideal
rows: Endurance. Hardship must be confronted unflinchingly, even in the face of great danger. (Neutral)
Beauty. The best response to joy or pain is to create beautiful things. (Good)
Honesty. Everything we do is seen by a higher being, so hiding anything is for fools. (Lawful)
Perspective. Everything looks different when you take the long view. (Any)
Cruelty. Weakness is a sin, and pain is the just and fair punishment for weakness. (Evil)
Chance. The greatest art—and adventure—comes from accident and discovery. (Chaos)

entries.caption: Bond
rows: I make art and seek out other artists wherever I go.
The world is to be experienced, in both terror and beauty.
A family member disappeared during a solitary vigil. I'm seeking them.
I want to prove that I can endure anything.
A saurian spirit-speaker connected me to the ghost of my wicked ancestor. I seek to atone for their wrongs.
I suffered terribly at the hands of a Lion Clan torturer. I seek to one day return and take my revenge.

entries.caption: Flaw
rows: I put myself in harm's way to prove that I can endure it.
I despise anyone who shows weakness in the face of suffering.
I hate myself for my inability to be completely stoic.
I truly feel nothing—and sometimes I wonder if that's a sign that something's wrong with me.
My interest in art isn't a passion… it's an obsession.
I am too slow to take action, believing that if we simply wait, everything will resolve itself in time.

entries: If I'm being honest, I both admire and fear the Lion Clan. Their art is breathtaking, their stoicism inspiring. But that refusal to express suffering—I fear it breeds cruelty and contempt for feeling, which is a trait more common to monsters than mortals.

items.name: Skill Proficiencies:
items.entry: {@skill Stealth}, {@skill Survival}

items.name: Tool Proficiencies:
items.entry: Gatherer's supplies

items.name: Languages:
items.entry: One of your choice

items.name: Equipment:
items.entry: A ceremonially broken symbol of your old community, a set of tattered clothes, a set of gatherer's supplies, and a hastily fashioned bundle containing a cooking knife, a small keepsake such as a carving or a necklace, ashes from the ceremonial fire, a ration of dried meat, and materials worth 5 ps in the Great Valley.

entries.name: Feature: Shelter-Finder
entries: You can easily find shelter in even adverse conditions, quickly creating a buffer between you and the elements. You know how to build shelters that are well concealed from predators and other sentient beings. While inside the shelter you are shielded from typical weather conditions such as rain, wind, snow, etc. These shelters can easily accommodate one creature of your own size, but with help and sufficient time and materials you can expand them to accommodate more or larger creatures.

entries.name: Suggested Characteristics
entries: Being cast out from everything you once knew changes a person. Some are made bitter by the change, others desire nothing more than to prove their innocence, while others simply move on. Use this table to inspire your character:

entries.caption: Personality Trait
rows: I often slip into reminiscences of my old life. As soon as I realize what I'm doing, I fall silent, embarrassed.
I avoid talking about my past by getting others to talk about themselves.
I don't judge others for their wrongdoing—even when I should.
I keep moving because the thought of putting down new roots is too painful.
I double check everything to avoid making mistakes.
I bond quickly with new people as a reaction to being alone against my will.
I like imperfect people. It's the ones who seem like they have it all together I don't trust.
I craft gifts for the people I miss back in my old community, and leave them by the side of the path.

entries.caption: Ideal
rows: Order. Rules were made to be followed, and those who break them should be punished. (Law)
Atonement. A person must repay their debts and restore the good faith they have broken. (Any)
Freedom. Rules and laws are unjust. People should do as they choose. (Chaotic)
Mercy. Mistakes are part of life, but grace can always be extended. (Good)
Survival. It doesn't matter what you do, everyone's just trying to stay alive. (Neutral)
Honor. Even when one's honor is broken, it still matters. (Good)

entries.caption: Bond
rows: I will one day return to my clan, having won back the right to be part of it.
Someone stood by my side while I was cast out. I'll never forget them.
A rival's scheme led to my exile. I'll pay them back even if it's the last thing I do.
The person who took me in when I was cast out is my true family.
I was outcast in pursuit of knowledge. I will continue to pursue that knowledge wherever it leads me, no matter what.
I fight for outcasts and exiles everywhere.

entries.caption: Flaw
rows: I think of myself as one already dead, and take foolish risks because my life ended the day I was exiled.
I doubt myself and hesitate at critical moments.
I mistrust authority and put my faith in those who show marks of previous punishment.
I intentionally cross lines trying to sabotage new relationships before I am rejected.
I act like everything's fine even when it's not.
I bury my unhappiness in food, strong drink, and violence.

items.name: Skill Proficiencies:
items.entry: {@skill Athletics}, {@skill Intimidation}

items.name: Tool Proficiencies:
items.entry: Vehicles (land), one type of gaming set

items.name: Equipment:
items.entry: A shield, a set of common clothes, a war-mask, and a belt pouch containing gems and carved ornaments worth up to 10 ps in the Great Valley.

entries.name: Feature: Known for Trouble
entries: People you encounter are unnerved and intimidated by your raider's swagger and the dangerous gleam in your eye. Unarmed strangers are hesitant to challenge you, even if you go where you aren't wanted, thanks to your bad reputation and obvious taste for trouble.

entries.name: Suggested Characteristics
entries: Raiders are an unwanted blight—a predatory, violent sort who take what others have worked for. Rowdy and uncouth, with no more culture than a pack of wolves, most raiders live for the moment, with no loyalty or creed beyond that of their pack.

entries.caption: Personality Trait
rows: I judge everyone's worth by whether or not I think I could take them in a fight.
My knuckles always itch just before a brawl.
I never saw a weapon I didn't like.
I hide a surprisingly dainty hobby from others.
I take great pains with my appearance and grooming.
I help myself to others' belongings out of sheer habit.
My favorite food is all of them.
I'm the best drinking buddy you've ever met.

entries.caption: Ideal
rows: Strength. The only law is strength—might is the ultimate arbiter of justice. (Neutral)
Mayhem. I thrive on upending order. (Chaotic)
Plunder. I love to steal. (Evil)
Loyalty. I serve those to whom I am sworn and none other. (Lawful)
Brotherhood. If you earn my trust, I'll stand by your side forever. (Good)
Violence. I thirst for bloodshed. (Evil)

entries.caption: Bond
rows: I stole from the wrong person, and have been hunted ever since.
Every night, before I sleep, I swear an oath to return to my old pack and kill the warlord I used to serve.
A raid gone wrong opened my eyes to a different way of life. I now seek to restore life wherever I can.
I'm still loyal to my old warlord. This work is at their command, and if they whistle, I'll run back to them.
I was raised with the raiders, and am only now discovering that there are other ways to live.
My old pack tossed me out. Said I was no good to them anymore. I'll prove them wrong.

entries.caption: Flaw
rows: My temper is so short you need a crafting hoop to find it.
I start fights for fun.
If there's food or drink, I'm eating or drinking it. All of it.
I can't help stealing objects I admire.
It's best to kill first and ask questions never.
I act rough and tumble to cover up my secret cowardice.

entries.name: Variant: Sharksail
entries: You're no common land raider—you're a sharksail, a proud pirate of the Scattersea. You've roved the waves, terrorizing the canoes and family boats of the Whale Clan, plundering villages and taking what you want for yourself. You may have even been inducted into the secretive Shark Clan, learning the name of their god, which is kept secret to outsiders.
If you decide to use this variant, change your proficiencies from Athletics to Nature and from land vehicles to sea vehicles.

items.name: Skill Proficiencies:
items.entry: {@skill Arcana}, {@skill Deception}

items.name: Tool Proficiencies:
items.entry: Mapmaker's tools

items.name: Languages:
items.entry: One of your choice

items.name: Equipment:
items.entry: A set of mapmaker's tools, a set of common clothes, a half-constructed talisman, and a belt pouch containing chalk, dried berries and meat, and semi-rare stones worth up to 10 ps in the Great Valley.

entries.name: Feature: Hidden Talent
entries: You know how to go about your work in secret. Even in a busy environment and under close scrutiny, given a little time, you can find somewhere out of sight—such as an abandoned structure, an unattended cave, or a quiet grove—in which to carry out experiments, preparations, training, and research.

entries.name: Suggested Characteristics
entries: Those who have had to conceal their secret knowledge or talents trust themselves above all. They may be eager to share what they know with willing listeners, or guarded, unwilling to speak of their discoveries, even to those they trust. Savants are, first and foremost, concerned with continuing to pursue that which they alone know to be true, and tend to be dedicated, focused, and intense.

entries.caption: Personality Trait
rows: I'm constantly scribbling diagrams in the dirt.
I forget to eat, sleep, and wash. I'm much too busy solving the great mysteries.
My vernacular is ornate and I have an inherent propensity towards embellished loquacity.
I don't bother explaining myself to lesser minds.
I smell faintly of brine and soot from the recent explosion my latest experiment caused.
I always answer a question with a question, don't you?
Everything is fascinating. And I mean everything.
I don't know how to dress properly, and often look mismatched and backwards.

entries.caption: Ideal
rows: Knowledge. I will not rest until I learn all I can. (Neutral)
Secrecy. Knowledge kept hidden cannot fall into the wrong hands. (Neutral)
Independence. I follow my own path, regardless of the will of others. (Chaotic)
Order. Only through structure can progress be achieved. (Lawful)
Ego. I alone perceive reality, and the fools who oppose me are less than nothing. (Evil)
Advancement. I seek to learn that I might better the lives of all. (Good)

entries.caption: Bond
rows: I am perilously close to a great and dangerous discovery.
After my experiments caused a terrible disaster, my clan ran me out into the wilderness.
A small and scattered group reveres me as their leader, and I must make contact with them to share my knowledge.
I learned something I long to forget, and adventure to get as far from it as possible.
My life's ambition is to find a certain expert on a matter of great importance to me and gain their insight.
I am determined to prove my genius once and for all, so that the whole world may know my brilliance.

entries.caption: Flaw
rows: I am, without question, the most brilliant person who ever lived. And I'll tell you so. Repeatedly.
I don't trust anyone—not after the way I was treated.
I refuse to let go of my pet theories, even when I have been proven wrong.
I overcomplicate everything.
I keep secrets from those closest to me.
I can't tell the difference between flattery and a forthright assessment of my genius.

entries: Many brilliant spellskins and notorious druids have started their lives in just such a fashion—their strangeness and unwillingness to be less than strange pushing them into genius… or destruction.

items.name: Skill Proficiencies:
items.entry: {@skill History}, {@skill Religion}

items.name: Tool Proficiencies:
items.entry: Ceremonial supplies

items.name: Languages:
items.entry: One of your choice

items.name: Equipment:
items.entry: A set of ceremonial supplies, a set of ceremonial clothes, a tiny fiend or celestial sealed in amber, and a belt pouch containing feathers, beads, twine, incense, and crystals worth up to 20 ps in the Great Valley.

entries.name: Feature: Well-Connected
entries: Growing up in Seerfall offered you the opportunity to interact with the powerful—both those who dwell there and those who made pilgrimages to the sanctuary to seek blessing or guidance. You know a powerful figure such as a chieftain, a high shaman, or a god. Work with your DM to determine who your connection is, but this person knows you, trusts you, and wishes to do you good, within the limits of their role and responsibilities.

entries.name: Suggested Characteristics
entries: They say that those who hail from Seerfall are either mystics or mockers. Growing up around so much divine magic either heightens your spirituality and sensitivity to wonder or utterly deadens it. Either way, Seerfall folk are said to be versed in lore and cunning, if a little entitled, thanks to the constant streams of offerings that make their way to the lonely sanctuary.

entries.caption: Personality Trait
rows: There is no moment or act too mundane for a prayer or blessing.
I can rattle off the names and symbols of so many gods that I'll bore my audience before I run out.
I'm intrigued by all matters of worship.
I'm always humming some ceremonial tune.
I have never touched a drop of alcohol.
I've seen every kind of divine rite there is, and nothing shocks me.
I grow bored quickly in serious moments.
My mystical open-mindedness makes me more than a little gullible.

entries.caption: Ideal
rows: Reverence. I bow to the greater powers because I know my place in the world. (Lawful)
Acceptance. Everything has a place in the spectrum of life and worship. (Neutral)
Faith. Belief is in itself a great power. (Good)
Discipline. I hold myself and others to a strict standard of life and conduct. (Lawful)
Control. I care only about the measure to which I can exert my will on others. (Evil)
Fun. Life is short and the gods are foolish, so let's have a good time while we can. (Chaotic)

entries.caption: Bond
rows: I am bound in the service of a secret cult.
I once disrupted a ceremony and unleashed a terrible fiend into the world.
I crossed the wrong shaman and have a curse laid upon me that I am trying to undo.
Having seen so much divine magic, I am traveling to learn more about the arcane.
I fell in love with a powerful guardian and seek to improve myself to be worthy of them.
A god marked me as their chosen one, but I ran away before the ceremony could be completed.

entries.caption: Flaw
rows: I am a hardened cynic, and find sincerity laughable.
My code of conduct is unrealistically rigid, for myself and my companions.
I find arcane magic unnatural and frightening.
I brag about my connections to everyone I meet.
My tendency is to over-spiritualize everything.
I believe myself to be much more wise and learned than I really am.

entries: Ah! Seerfall. How I love it. Have you walked its misty corridors? Have you seen the haunted halls and heard the songs of a dozen gods echoing through the chimes of gathered shamans? It is a most sacred and serene place... if complicated and perhaps a little dangerous to outsiders who take a wrong turn.

items.name: Skill Proficiencies:
items.entry: {@skill History}, {@skill Performance}

items.name: Tool Proficiencies:
items.entry: One type of musical instrument

items.name: Languages:
items.entry: One of your choice

items.name: Equipment:
items.entry: A musical instrument of your choice, a set of common clothes, a pouch of sands that can cause a fire to flare various colors, and a belt pouch containing costume jewelry and small whistles and chimes worth up to 15 ps in the Great Valley.

entries.name: Feature: Welcome at the Fire
entries: You make your way by telling stories, and those who hear you speak recognize your gift with words. You can entertain an audience for an evening, and be rewarded with food, water, and a comfortable place to sleep. Additionally, people are eager to share their stories with you, and you find it easy to pick up rumors and tall tales, given a few hours in a new place.

entries.name: Suggested Characteristics
entries: Storytellers are typically a flamboyant folk who are most at ease when the eyes of all are upon them. The typical storyteller is gregarious, spontaneous, and enjoys the attention and the flattery of others—although whether that's their true self or merely the face they show the world is known only to the storyteller and those to whom they choose to tell the truth.

entries.caption: Personality Trait
rows: I always dress to capture attention.
Once I start a story, nothing can stop me from seeing it through to the end.
There's nothing I love more than hearing someone else's tale.
I am an excellent toast-giver.
I believe there's nothing more life-affirming than a well-placed compliment.
I ask a lot of questions of strangers—how else will I hear new stories?
My attention wanders quickly if I'm not entertained.
I treat everyone as my very best friend.

entries.caption: Ideal
rows: Happiness. Nothing matters more than seeing joy spread. (Good)
Art. I live for the craft. (Any)
Tradition. I pass down the stories and beliefs I inherited. (Lawful)
Creativity. Spontaneity and improvisation are essential in all of life, not just stories. (Chaos)
People. I seek connection with others, without judgment. (Neutral)
Manipulation. I control others with my words to get what I want. (Evil)

entries.caption: Bond
rows: I seek to live the greatest story of all time.
I am convinced of the truth of a tale that everyone else tells me is just a fable.
There are many encampments where I have slipped away from romantic entanglements.
My words once inspired a clan to rise up against an evil chieftain, and I am a hero to that clan.
I don't know where the stories I tell come from. It's as if I'm possessed when I begin speaking to a crowd.
I once betrayed a dear friend by sharing their secret as a story, and I carry that guilt with me always.

entries.caption: Flaw
rows: I cannot keep a secret.
I can't tell fact from fiction, and am usually lying about something.
I put others in harm's way to see what will happen.
I always tell others what I think they want to hear.
My appetite for carousing continually gets me into difficult situations.
Some say I'm too dramatic, but that's just because they hate me and don't understand me.

items.name: Skill Proficiencies:
items.entry: {@skill Insight}, {@skill Persuasion}

items.name: Languages:
items.entry: Two of your choice

items.name: Equipment:
items.entry: A set of counting sticks, a set of common clothes, a trinket from your first major trade, and a belt pouch containing gems, salt, and baubles worth up to 25 ps in the Great Valley.

entries.name: Feature: Set Up Shop
entries: It's the most natural thing in the world for you to begin trading when you come to a new place. You can offer goods and services for barter in a shared space such as the edge of a clanfire, the fields outside an encampment, or a public market. Those in authority will assume you are legitimate, and you can usually trade whatever you have for items of slightly greater value, given sufficient time to negotiate.

entries.name: Suggested Characteristics
entries: In a world constantly on the move, traders are seen as the greatest of travelers, and are assumed to be wise in the ways of the world and knowledgeable about its rumors and news. Traders tend to be likable, easygoing folk, and know how to read others and make them feel important while walking away with the better end of a deal.

entries.caption: Personality Trait
rows: I flatter others as a matter of habit.
I'm always swapping one thing for another.
I collect objects as I travel, even if I have no use for them.
I insist on everything being perfectly fair at all times.
I sleep on a pile of my goods and wares.
If there's a rumor of something strange, unusual, or valuable, I have to investigate it.
I love to ask people where they acquired everything they're wearing or carrying.
I know people everywhere I go.

entries.caption: Ideal
rows: Fairness. All things should be perfectly balanced. (Neutral)
Help. Happiness comes from bettering the lives of others with a good trade. (Good)
Greed. I covet that which others possess. (Evil)
Profit. If I don't walk away with more than I started, what's the point? (Neutral)
Honor. A person is only as good as their reputation. (Lawful)
Trickery. A good deal is whatever you can get away with. (Chaotic)

entries.caption: Bond
rows: I protect my reputation above all.
There may or may not be a large number of settlements where I swapped less-than-legitimate goods.
I am in love with the son or daughter of an important chieftain, and strive to earn enough to deserve them.
I swapped for a truly strange object and have become obsessed with learning its purpose.
A long time ago, I bartered part of my soul away.
I recently came across rumors of a great windfall and am eager to profit from it.

entries.caption: Flaw
rows: There's no such thing as enough, and I will never be satisfied.
I have a mercenary soul, and will do anything as long as it's profitable.
I think of people only in terms of their value to me.
My love for fleecing the ignorant gets me into a lot of trouble.
I have become addicted to a certain rare substance that I obtained in a trade long ago.
I care more about possessions than lives.

entries: Traders have a special seat at the clanfire of my heart. I love their earnest ways, their cunning conversation, and their traveler's tales. Give me a trader on any adventure. They'll be good company and we'll all come away with a bit more salt in our belt pouches.

items.name: Skill Proficiencies:
items.entry: {@skill Acrobatics}, {@skill Nature}

items.name: Tool Proficiencies:
items.entry: Navigator's tools, vehicles (water)

items.name: Equipment:
items.entry: A set of navigator's tools, a set of common clothes, a large shell that whispers when you hold it to your ear, and a belt pouch containing shells, coral, shark teeth, and sea urchin spines worth up to 15 ps in the Great Valley.

entries.name: Feature: Boat-Born
entries: You are at ease on the water, feeling more comfortable on the sea than on land. You know how to cross most bodies of water safely, or are aware of the kinds of dangers that treacherous water might contain. It's easy to strike up conversation with others who live near the water, and if you encounter fishers, boaters, or other coastal people, they are inclined to trust you and see you as one of their own.

entries.name: Suggested Characteristics
entries: The Whale Clan is known for their love of family and love of questions. Often, those who hail from the Scattersea seek close connection with others, forging friendships through deep curiosity, both about the lives of others and what might be over the next wave. Whale Clan members can come off as too inquisitive, both of others and of a dangerous world, where putting one's hand into a hole to find out what's inside often results in lost fingers.

entries.caption: Personality Trait
rows: I'm constantly pulling strange sea life out of my attire.
Why should I ever stop asking questions?
Men are nice to look at, but they should keep quiet while the women are speaking.
I can't stand still, I'm always moving.
There's no difference between friends and family—I'd die for either.
I don't stop to think, preferring to charge ahead and sort out the consequences later.
I'm easily distracted by anything I don't understand.
I find land without water unnerving and unnatural.

entries.caption: Ideal
rows: Family. Blood is the highest law. (Lawful)
Respect. Honor keeps individuals, families, and traditions alive. (Lawful)
Freedom. Everyone should go where they will and do as they please. (Neutral)
Curiosity. A life without questions is no life at all. (Neutral)
Abandonment. Those outside the family exist merely to be used and left behind. (Evil)
Adventure. What do we live for but to seize that which lies beyond the next curve of the coast? (Any)

entries.caption: Bond
rows: I set out to form a new family, in accordance with the tradition of my people.
Something terrible in an abandoned village set me on my current course.
A merfolk uttered a great mystery to me, which I am determined to solve.
I was marooned as punishment for a crime. I am seeking to return to my family, for restoration or revenge… I'm not sure which.
A magical effect cast me far from my home. I am determined to return to it.
I'm seeking a strange sorcerer I met at sea who promised me the answer to all my questions if I would come and find them.

entries.caption: Flaw
rows: What do you mean I ask too many questions? Which ones? These ones? Hey, where are you going?
I'm never satisfied anywhere, always itching to get on to the next thing.
I run from any situation I find uncomfortable.
I treat most people as disposable. I'm only interested in them as long as they serve my purpose.
I get land-sick if I spend too much time away from the water.
I habitually lie to set others at ease, and rarely say what I really think.

spell.name: Aggravate Wounds

components.m: a smear of blood, a pinch of salt

entries: Weaving a painful blood curse, you choose a creature within range that you can see. That creature must make a Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, whenever the affected target takes bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing damage, the target takes an additional {@damage 1d6} necrotic damage.

entriesHigherLevel.name: At Higher Levels
entries: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 4th level or higher, increase the damage by {@dice 1d6} for each two spell levels above 3rd.

spell.name: Bite Back

time.condition: which you take in response to being damaged by a melee attack

entries: When you are damaged by a melee attack, a spiritual beast rises from the wound to sink its teeth into the creature that harmed you. The creature must make a Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save it takes {@damage 2d8} piercing damage, and is restrained until the end of your next turn if it is no larger than one size bigger than you. On a successful save, it takes half damage and is not restrained.

spell.name: Bolt of Ush

components.m: a sapphire worth 2,000 ps or spilled with 2,000 hp of blood

entries: You summon a storm directly overhead, flashing with magical lightning. At your command, the lightning strikes a sphere with a radius of 20 feet, centered on you. You and up to 8 willing creatures in range are magically transported to a location with which you are familiar on the same plane. Your familiarity with the destination determines whether you arrive there successfully as by the {@spell teleport} spell. You arrive via lightning strike at your chosen location. The spell fails if you can't see a point in the air where the storm cloud could appear, or if the destination lacks a similar point in the air.

Any creatures within a 20-foot sphere centered on you when you land must make a Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save they take {@damage 10d8} lightning damage, and half as much on a successful save. Any structures in range take 50 points of lightning damage, and flammable objects in range ignite.
None of the creatures transported by the spell are harmed by the lightning, although you smell faintly of ozone for 10 minutes.

spell.name: Bury
entries: Choose a creature you can see within range that is within 5 feet of the ground. The creature must succeed on a Strength saving throw. On a failure, the creature takes {@damage 6d8} bludgeoning damage and is forced just below the surface of the ground. On a success, the target takes half damage and is knocked prone. A target standing on stone or a similar solid material automatically succeeds on their saving throw.

While buried the target is restrained, blinded, deafened, and unable to breathe. The target or an allied creature can spend an action on their turn to make a Strength check versus your spell save DC, digging the target out on a success. If the target has a burrowing speed, they automatically succeed on their Strength check to escape.

spell.name: Deflect Magic

time.condition: when you succeed on a saving throw against a spell targeting only you

entries: You attempt to reflect a spell that targets you back towards its caster. If you are the only target of a spell of 5th level or lower, the spell has no effect on you, and instead targets the caster, using the slot level, spell save DC, attack bonus, and spellcasting ability of the caster.

If the spell targeting you is of 6th level or higher, make an ability check using your spellcasting ability. The DC equals 10 + the spell's level. On a success, the creature's spell fails and has no effect.

entriesHigherLevel.name: At Higher Levels
entries: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 6th level or higher, the spell targeting you is reflected if its level is less than or equal to the level of the spell slot you used. When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 7th level or higher, you can choose the target of the reflected spell as if the spell was cast from your location.

spell.name: Disorient
entries: Choose up to 10 creatures you can see within range. Targeted creatures must make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failure, until the spell ends, a creature is magically lost, and unable to find their way to any intended location unless they can see it. They have disadvantage on Wisdom and Intelligence checks to gain their bearings or discern their current location.

While affected by the spell, if a creature attempts to cast a spell that allows them to magically find their way, they encounter a mental block and must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw versus your spell save DC or their spell fails.
This spell can be ended by restorative magic like {@spell lesser restoration} or {@spell dispel magic}.

entriesHigherLevel.name: At Higher Levels
entries: If you cast this spell using a spell slot of 5th level or higher, the duration is 24 hours. If you use a spell slot of 6th level or higher, the duration is 1 week. If you use a spell slot of 7th level or higher, the duration is 1 month. If you use a spell slot of 8th level or higher, the duration is 1 year. If you use a 9th-level spell slot, the spell lasts until it is dispelled.

spell.name: Dream of Kho

components.m: a carved ivory drumstick and a drum worth 300 ps or with 300 hp worth of blood spilled on it

entries: You drum a hypnotic rhythm, weaving a dream state around all willing creatures within range. As the tempo rises towards a crescendo, willing creatures begin to see a phantom forest sprout around them. The forest conforms to the shape of the space the creatures inhabit, but may introduce phantom obstacles such as large trees, creeks or waterfalls, rock piles, and so on. These are real for creatures affected by the spell. Somewhere within range is a huge celestial ape. The ape understands all languages, but does not speak.

The ape is capable of summoning any beast or object from the same plane that is not bound by magic or currently attuned to another person. This includes generic beasts or objects such as "cat" or "sword," or named requests such as "the cat Skelka" or "Fangblade." However, the ape is under no compulsion to behave in any particular way, and will usually not agree to summon anything unless he is satisfied that he has been offered something in fair payment.

If the ape is especially pleased with the payment offered, he may summon the desired object and additional items as well. If he is displeased, he may fly into a rage and attack. All creatures affected by the spell must make a DC 18 Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, creatures take {@damage 8d10} radiant damage and cease to be affected by the spell. On a successful save, creatures take half damage.

spell.name: Form Weapon
entries: You reach out and summon a weapon from the environment around you. Raw materials uproot themselves and form in your hands into a magical simple weapon of your choice with which you have proficiency. The style of weapon reflects the materials in the environment, creating a bone sword in a graveyard or a grass sling in an open field, for example. You can only have one weapon formed by this spell at a time. When the spell ends, the weapon falls apart.

spell.name: Fury of Twr

components.m: sulfur, ash, an ox horn worth 100 ps or spilled with 100 hp of blood, which is consumed by the spell

entries: You unleash a fury of fiendish rage all around you. All creatures within range must make a Dexterity saving throw. Each creature takes {@damage 5d10} fire damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

All water in range immediately boils and releases jets of superheated steam, which last for the spell's duration, and any creature that ends its turn in or above more than 1 inch of water must make a Dexterity save. The creature takes {@damage 3d10} fire damage from the superheated steam on a failed save, or half as much on a successful one.

spell.name: Gaze of Glelh

components.m: an ivory mortar & pestle, and colorful pigments worth 200 ps, which are consumed by the spell

entries: You anoint a ceremonial square 10 feet in diameter with bright colors and symbols of a lion with wide, staring eyes. For the spell's duration, any creature within the ceremonial square can see everything up to 10 miles away within line of sight of the square with telescopic vision of perfect clarity, no matter how small or far away.

Creatures within 10 miles may be drawn to the ceremonial square by the spell's power. As an action, you can target a creature within range. Targeted creatures must make a Wisdom saving throw, and targets who can see the ceremonial square or creatures or structures inside it make their saving throw with disadvantage. On a failure, they must use their movement to approach the ceremonial square and, upon reaching the edge of the square, lie paralyzed next to it until the spell ends.
The spell ends if the ceremonial square contains no conscious creatures with a Wisdom score of 4 or greater.

spell.name: Heart of Urhosh

time.condition: when you or a creature you can see within range would drop to 0 hit points or die

entries: You cry out to spirits of survival to preserve the life of a creature you can see within range. The creature drops to 1 hit point instead of dying. Until the end of the target's next turn, they cannot be reduced to below 1 hit point. In addition, effects that would kill the target instantaneously have no effect.

spell.name: Lava Leap
entries: You summon the fury of the volcano to hurl yourself into the air. When you cast this spell, you can make a standing leap to any point you can see within range. Immediately after you leap, a surge of magma explodes outward, and each creature in a 15-foot radius of the place you just left must make a Dexterity saving throw, taking {@damage 3d10} fire damage on a failed save and half as much on a successful one.
The magma cools instantly, but the cooled magma makes the ground difficult terrain in a 30-foot-diameter circle around the spot you left.

spell.name: Mage Skis
entries: You summon long strips of arcane energy that grant you the ability to move quickly over frozen terrain. Until the spell ends, up to 6 willing creatures you can see within range gain a speed of 60 feet when traveling over snowy or frozen terrain. Frozen or snowy terrain is not considered difficult terrain for anyone affected by this spell.

entriesHigherLevel.name: At Higher Levels
entries: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 3rd level or higher, you can target one additional creature for each spell level above 2nd.

spell.name: Nightmare Bind
entries: You choose a sleeping creature within range, attempting to sink them deeper into sleep while troubling their mind with dark dreams. The creature must make a Wisdom saving throw. On a successful save, the target awakes. On a failure, the target is trapped in restless sleep until the spell ends, and does not gain the benefits of a long rest. You do not choose the content of their dreams, nor are you aware of their nature. Each time the target takes damage, it makes a new Wisdom saving throw against the spell. If the saving throw succeeds, the spell ends.

entriesHigherLevel.name: At Higher Levels
entries: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 3rd or 4th level, the spell lasts for up to 24 hours. When you use a spell slot of 5th level or 6th level, the spell lasts for up to 1 week. When you use a spell slot of 7th level or 8th level, the spell lasts for up to 1 month. When you use a spell slot of 9th level, the spell lasts until dispelled.

spell.name: Possess Steed
entries: You and up to 10 willing creatures each touch a separate willing steed of at least one size larger than yourself. All affected creatures become immaterial spirits, along with all their carried gear, and possess their steeds, controlling them. Until the spell ends, affected creatures use the steed's Strength, Dexterity, and Constitution scores, but retain their own Intelligence, Wisdom, and Charisma scores.
The steeds outwardly appear unaffected by this change, and the possession cannot be detected by visual inspection of their outward appearance, although unusual behavior may raise suspicion.
If an affected creature's steed drops to 0 hit points, the creature reverts to their physical form within 5 feet of the steed.

spell.name: Quillburst
entries: Arcane quills bristle around you, then burst outward in sudden violence. Each creature in a 20-foot sphere centered on you must make a Dexterity saving throw, taking {@damage 2d6} piercing damage on a failed save and half as much on a successful one.
Until the spell ends, if you would take damage from an attack, you can release a second burst with the same effects as the first, ending the spell.

spell.name: Ridicule
entries: You ridicule a humanoid you can see within range, lacing your words with magical disdain. The target must succeed on a Charisma saving throw. On a failed save, the target has disadvantage on Charisma checks until the spell ends, and others who encounter them will find them laughable, though they may not express it outwardly if the target is in a position of authority over them.

spell.name: Sense Whispers
entries: You listen carefully for the subtle whispers of unseen spirits. Within range, you sense the location of any friendly undead creatures.

spell.name: Smoke Breathing
entries: This spell grants up to ten willing creatures you can see within range the ability to breathe nonmagical harmful gas until the spell ends. Affected creatures also retain their normal mode of respiration. Additionally, until the spell ends, affected creatures can exhale smoke at will as a harmless visual effect. This smoke dissipates immediately and does not provide cover.

spell.name: Steal Speed
entries: A creature you can see within range must make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failure, their speed is halved, and any speeds you possess are increased by the amount by which their speed is reduced.

spell.name: Song of Mala

components.m: an oyster shell containing a pearl worth 750 ps or spilled with 750 hp of blood

entries: You raise your voice, and begin to sing with the blessing of divine spirits. Your song is magically enhanced and carries over incredible distances.

You can choose to have your song heard by all creatures within range, any number of specific creatures within range, or any number of specific type(s) of creatures you select within range. (For example, the song might be heard by every undead creature and every elf; or every undead elf.)
Using this song you can communicate a message up to 25 words, which will be understood by any hearer regardless of what languages they speak (as long as they speak at least one language).
As long as you concentrate on this spell, any creature that heard your song can pinpoint your location.

spell.name: Spellblinding

components.m: wool, feathers, rare inks worth 1,000 ps or mixed with 1,000 hp of spilled blood, which is consumed by the spell

entries: You choose a creature or object in range that you can see, and render it invisible to spellcasters. Any creature capable of casting a cantrip or spell of 1st-level or higher is unable to see the target you choose until the spell ends, nor can they be detected by spellcasters using magical means. Creatures who cannot cast cantrips or spells can see the target as before.

spell.name: Stonebone
entries: You touch a willing creature. Their bones harden and become dense as stone, and their knuckles, elbows, shoulders, and knees sprout rocky protrusions. Until the spell ends, they have resistance to bludgeoning damage, and their unarmed attacks deal an extra {@damage 1d4} bludgeoning damage.

spell.name: Stormscrying

components.m: a needle and a jar of water

entries: You name a location with which you are familiar. You sense the current weather at that location, detecting temperature, precipitation, wind, time of day, and so on. If you use this spell to detect the weather of a large region, you gain a more general indication of the weather, such as "scattered thunderstorms" or "partly foggy."

spell.name: Tilt
entries: Choose up to 8 willing creatures within range. Until the spell ends, affected creatures' sense of gravity shifts by up to 90 degrees in the direction of your choice. Affected creatures fall in your chosen direction, as does any gear worn or carried by affected creatures at the time of casting. As an action, you can change the direction of gravity for all affected creatures while the spell is active.

spell.name: Time Slip
entries: A creature you can see within range must make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failure, they become confused about time, believing it to be whatever hour of the day you choose. They remain convinced of the time until the spell ends, rationalizing explanations for unusual darkness or light based on the enchantments of the spell.

spell.name: Traitor's Kiss

components.m: a dab of paint and a serpent's tongue

entries: You touch a willing creature and grant them the appearance of another's trusted companion. Choose a humanoid with whom you are familiar, and give your target the appearance, voice, and scent of their most trusted humanoid friend, relative, or confidante. The DM determines the form based on who the target is most likely to trust. The target has advantage on Charisma checks to influence the chosen humanoid, and magically learns general information about the illusory persona, including their name, relation to the target, and personality.

spell.name: Weight
entries: Choose an object in range no larger than a 5-foot cube. You cause the object to become unbearably heavy. The object does not bounce, roll, or slide unless it is on a steep incline. A creature can attempt a Strength check against your spell save DC to lift the object, ending the spell on a success.

If you target an object held or worn by a hostile creature, that creature must succeed on a Strength saving throw to avoid the spell. On a failure, the creature takes {@damage 2d6} bludgeoning damage, dropping the object if it is held, or falling prone if it is worn.

entriesHigherLevel.name: At Higher Levels
entries: When you cast this spell at higher levels, you can target objects of an additional 5 feet in size per spell level above 2nd.

baseitem.name: Antlerclaw
entries: A sharpened antler with leather grips.

baseitem.name: Gripstone
entries: A handheld stone with grooves for fingerholds, used as a striking tool or weapon.

baseitem.name: Atlatl
entries: Also known as a spear-thrower, this short shaft features a cup or spur at the end where the butt of a javelin or spear is placed, allowing the wielder to launch the projectile farther and straighter than if thrown by hand.

baseitem.name: Clubsword
entries: A thick club with jagged stones, teeth, or shards of obsidian lining its edges, which can be used to smash or cut by a skilled wielder.

baseitem.name: Hammerstone
entries: Also known as a hav or lifting stone, this stone sphere is often used in mammoth hunts, hurled or dropped from a height onto prey, or thrown with great force from a short distance.

baseitem.name: Saw
entries: A serrated blade, used to hook and tear at wood, harvested prey, or enemies. It deals {@dice 1d6} piercing damage or {@dice 1d6} slashing damage, at the wielder's choice.

baseitem.name: Warclub
entries: A huge bludgeoning weapon, usually made from a combination of bone, leather, wood, and stone.

baseitem.name: Bolas
entries: Two or more stones attached by a length of cord (usually leather) used to entangle prey from a distance.

entries.name: Special
entries: A Large or smaller creature hit by bolas is {@condition restrained} until it is freed. Bolas have no effect on creatures that are formless, or creatures that are Huge or larger. A creature can use its action to make a {@dc 10} Strength check, freeing itself or another creature within its reach on a success. Dealing 2 slashing damage to the bolas (AC 8) also frees the creature without harming it, ending the effect and destroying the bolas.
When you use an action, bonus action, or reaction to attack with bolas, you can make only one attack regardless of the number of attacks you can normally make.

baseitem.name: Boomerang
entries: A flat, curved club, thrown in an arc.

entries.name: Special
entries: When you miss on an attack with the boomerang, it returns to you at the end of your turn unless its returning path is heavily obstructed.

item.name: Magic Mask
entries: This mask is carved and decorated to resemble a specific creature, and imbues its wearer with a measure of that creature's power. As a bonus action, you can put on or remove the mask. The mask has 5 charges. While wearing the mask, as an action you expend 1 of its charges and immediately take the mask's action. If the action uses an item such as a weapon or other held object, the mask projects a spectral version of the object, which disappears when the charge is expended.
The mask regains 1d4+1 expended charges daily at dawn. If you expend the mask's last charge, roll a d20. On a 1, the mask's magic fades and it becomes a mundane object.

item.name: Mask of the Ape
entries: This huge, glowering mask allows you to hurl spectral boulders. When you expend a charge, make a ranged weapon attack against a target you can see within 50 feet, or within 100 feet with disadvantage. This attack is +9 to hit. On a hit, deal 7d6 + 6 bludgeoning damage.

item.name: Mask of the Balor
entries: This red, ox-headed mask unleashes an aura of fire around you. When you expend a charge, each creature within 5 feet of you takes 3d6 fire damage, and flammable objects in the aura that aren't being worn or carried ignite. Until the start of your next turn, a creature that touches you or hits you with a melee attack while within 5 feet of you takes 3d6 fire damage.

item.name: Mask of the Bat
entries: This crude mask with oversized ears grants you the senses of the bat. When you expend a charge, you gain blindsight to a range of 60 feet until the start of your next turn.

item.name: Mask of the Blink Dog
entries: This mask of a sharp-eared canine allows you to teleport. When you expend a charge as an action, you magically teleport, along with any equipment you are wearing or carrying, up to 40 feet to an unoccupied space you can see. This teleportation does not provoke attacks of opportunity. As part of this action, either before or after teleporting, you can make one attack.

item.name: Mask of the Cobra
entries: This fanged, hooded mask gives you the power of a poisonous bite. When you expend a charge, make a melee weapon attack against a target you can see within 10 feet. The attack is +6 to hit, and deals 1d4 + 4 piercing damage. On a hit, the target must make a DC 11 Constitution saving throw, taking 3d6 poison damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

item.name: Mask of the Dragon
entries: This red, polished mask of a great dragon grants you fiery breath. When you expend a charge, you exhale fire in a 30-foot cone. Each creature in that area must make a DC 17 Dexterity saving throw, taking 16d6 fire damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

item.name: Mask of the Duergar
entries: This gray, dwarvish mask grants you the power to enlarge your form. When you expend a charge, you magically increase in size, along with anything you are wearing or carrying, for 1 minute, during which time you cannot expend another charge with this mask. Your size category increases by 1. While enlarged, you double your damage dice on Strength-based weapon attacks, and make Strength checks and Strength saving throws with advantage. If you lack the room to increase by 1 size, you attain the maximum size possible in the space available.

item.name: Mask of the Eagle
entries: This mask features the sharp hooked beak and yellow eyes of an eagle. When you expend a charge, you gain a fly speed equal to your walking speed until the start of your next turn.

item.name: Mask of the Lion
entries: This golden-maned mask grants the predator's mighty pounce. If you move at least 20 feet straight toward a creature and then hit it with a melee attack on the same turn, you may expend a charge as part of your attack action. The target must succeed on a DC 13 Strength saving throw or be knocked prone. If the target is prone, you can make one additional melee attack against it as a bonus action.

item.name: Mask of the Mimic
entries: This amorphous mask allows you to disguise yourself as an object. When you expend a charge, you can turn into an object of approximately the same size as yourself. While you remain motionless, you are indistinguishable from an ordinary object, although you still retain all your existing stats, abilities, proficiencies, and requirements (such as food, air, and sleep). You can use your bonus action to return to your own form.

item.name: Mask of the Spider
entries: This black, many-eyed mask gives you the power to shoot webbing. When you expend a charge, make a ranged weapon attack against a creature you can see within 30 feet, or 60 feet with disadvantage. The attack is +5 to hit. On a hit, the target is restrained by webbing. As an action, the restrained target can make a DC 12 Strength check, bursting the webbing on a success. The webbing can also be attacked and destroyed (AC 10; hp 5; vulnerability to fire damage; immunity to bludgeoning, poison, and psychic damage).

item.name: Mask of the Sprite
entries: This wide-eyed, glittering mask grants the power to see into a creature's heart. When you expend a charge, you can touch a creature and magically know the creature's current emotional state. If the target fails a DC 10 Charisma saving throw, you also know the creature's alignment (if any). Celestials, fiends, and undead automatically fail the saving throw.

item.name: Mask of the Storm Giant
entries: This frightening, oversized mask allows you to wield the power of lightning. When you expend a charge, you hurl a magical lightning bolt at a point you can see within 500 feet of it. Each creature within 10 feet of that point must make a DC 17 Dexterity saving throw, taking 12d8 lightning damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

item.name: Mask of the Toad
entries: This brown, goggle-eyed mask features an enormous bite. When you expend a charge, make a melee weapon attack against a target you can see. The bite is +4 to hit. On a hit, deal 1d10 + 2 piercing damage plus 1d10 poison damage, and the target is grappled (escape DC 13). Until this grapple ends, the target is restrained, and you can't bite another target.

item.name: Mask of the Treant
entries: This wooden, branched mask allows you to animate trees. When you expend a charge, you magically animate one or two trees you can see within 60 feet. These trees have the statistics of a treant, except they have Intelligence and Charisma scores of 1, they can't speak, and they have only the Slam action option. An animated tree acts as your ally. The tree remains animate for 1 day or until it dies; until you die or are more than 120 feet from the tree; or until you take a bonus action to turn it back into an inanimate tree. The tree then takes root if possible. You cannot expend another charge with this mask while any tree is animated by its magic.

item.name: Mask of the Unicorn
entries: This mask of purest white with a spiraling horn grants you a healing touch. When you expend a charge, you can touch another creature with the mask's horn. The target magically regains 2d8 + 2 hit points. In addition, the touch removes all diseases and neutralizes all poisons afflicting the target.

item.name: Mask of the Vampire
entries: This pale, fanged mask summons the children of the night at your command. When you expend a charge, you magically call 2d4 swarms of bats or rats, provided that the sun isn't up. While outdoors, you can call 3d6 wolves instead. The called creatures arrive in 1d4 rounds, acting as your allies and obeying your spoken commands. The beasts remain for 1 hour, until you die, or until you dismiss them as a bonus action.

item.name: Mask of the Wraith
entries: This translucent, ghostly mask grants you the power of incorporeal movement. When you expend a charge, until the start of your next turn, you can move through other creatures and objects as if they were difficult terrain. Take 1d10 force damage if you end your turn inside an object.

item.name: Amulet of the Arena
entries: While wearing this amulet, your size doubles in all dimensions, and your weight is multiplied by eight. This growth increases your size by one category—from Medium to Large, for example. If there isn't enough room to double your size, you take 1d6 force damage per 5 feet of constrained space, and are restrained until you remove the amulet or are freed from the space. Until the amulet is removed, you also have advantage on Strength checks and Strength saving throws. Your weapons also grow to match your new size.

item.name: Apparatus of the Hawk
entries: This item first appears to be a Large sealed stone cylinder weighing 400 pounds. The cylinder has a hidden catch, which can be found with a successful DC 20 Intelligence (Investigation) check. Releasing the catch unlocks a hatch at one end of the monument, allowing two Medium or smaller creatures to crawl inside. Ten levers are set in a row at the far end, each in a neutral position, able to move either up or down. When certain levers are used, the apparatus transforms to resemble a giant hawk.
The Apparatus of the Hawk is a Large object with the following statistics:

items.name: Speed:
items.entry: 10 ft., Fly 60 ft. (or 0 ft. if the talons or wings aren't extended)

items.name: Damage Immunities:
items.entry: poison, psychic

entries: To be used as a vehicle, the apparatus requires one pilot. While the apparatus' hatch is closed, the compartment is airtight and warm. The compartment holds enough air for 10 hours of breathing, divided by the number of breathing creatures inside.
The apparatus is capable of flight unless it is submerged in liquid or its wings are restrained or blocked by obstacles.

A creature in the compartment can use an action to move as many as two of the apparatus' levers up or down. After each use, a lever goes back to its neutral position. Each lever, from left to right, functions as shown in the Apparatus of the Hawk Levers table.

entries.caption: Apparatus of the Hawk Levers
rows: Wings extend, allowing the apparatus to fly.
Wings retract, reducing the apparatus' flying speed to 0 and making it unable to benefit from bonuses to speed.
Forward window shutter opens.
Forward window shutter closes.
Floor and ceiling window shutters open (one above, one below).
Floor and ceiling window shutters close (one above, one below).
Two talons extend from the underside side of the apparatus, raising it 5 ft. and allowing it to walk.
The talons retract, reducing the apparatus's walking speed to 0 and making it unable to benefit from bonuses to speed.
The apparatus walks or flies forward, if talons or wings are extended.
The apparatus walks or flies backward, if talons or wings are extended.
Each extended wing makes the following melee weapon attack: +8 to hit, reach 20 ft. Hit: 7 (2d6) bludgeoning damage.
The talons make the following melee weapon attack: +8 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: The target is grappled (escape DC 15)
The apparatus turns 90 degrees left.
The apparatus turns 90 degrees right.
Lenses cover over the windows, magnifying objects viewed through them to twice their size.
The lenses retract.
The apparatus descends as much as 20 feet in air.
The apparatus rises up to 20 feet in air.
The rear hatch unseals and opens.
The rear hatch closes and seals.

item.name: Apparatus of the Mammoth
entries: This item first appears to be a Huge sealed stone monument weighing 10,000 pounds, featuring the carved head of a mammoth and two stone tusks. The monument has a hidden catch, which can be found with a successful DC 20 Intelligence (Investigation) check. Releasing the catch unlocks a hatch at one end of the monument, allowing six Large or smaller creatures to crawl inside. Ten levers are set in a row at the far end, each in a neutral position, able to move either up or down. When certain levers are used, the apparatus transforms to resemble a stone mammoth.
The Apparatus of the Mammoth is a Huge object with the following statistics:

items.name: Speed:
items.entry: 30 ft., Swim 10 ft. (or 0 ft. if the legs aren't extended)

items.name: Damage Immunities:
items.entry: poison, psychic

entries: To be used as a vehicle, the apparatus requires one pilot. While the apparatus's hatch is closed, the compartment is airtight. The compartment holds enough air for 10 hours of breathing, divided by the number of breathing creatures inside.
The apparatus takes no penalty to speed from difficult terrain.

A creature in the compartment can use an action to move as many as two of the apparatus' levers up or down. After each use, a lever goes back to its neutral position. Each lever, from left to right, functions as shown in the Apparatus of the Mammoth Levers table.

entries.caption: Apparatus of the Mammoth Levers
rows: Legs extend, allowing the apparatus to walk.
Legs retract, reducing the apparatus's speed to 0 and making it unable to benefit from bonuses to speed.
Forward window shutter opens.
Forward window shutter closes.
Side window shutters open (one per side).
Side window shutters close (one per side).
A trunklike appendage uncurls from the front of the apparatus.
The trunk retracts.
The apparatus walks forward, if legs are already extended.
The apparatus walks backward, if legs are already extended.
The tusks make the following melee weapon attack: +8 to hit, reach 20 ft. Hit: 18 (4d8) bludgeoning damage.
The extended trunk makes the following melee weapon attack: +8 to hit, reach 20 ft., one target. Hit: The target is grappled (escape DC 15)
The apparatus turns 90 degrees left, if legs are extended.
The apparatus turns 90 degrees right, if legs are extended.
The trunk emits a loud trumpeting that can be heard clearly for 1 mile, and faintly for 5 miles.
The trunk emits a loud trumpeting that can be heard clearly for 1 mile, and faintly for 5 miles.

If the legs are extended, the apparatus rears on its hind legs briefly, before slamming back down in a stomp attack: +8 to hit, reach 5 ft. Hit: 55 (10d10) bludgeoning damage. Creatures in a 10 ft. radius must succeed on a DC 15 Dexterity saving throw or be knocked prone.

If the legs are extended, the apparatus charges forward up to 60 feet. Creatures in its path must make a DC 15 Dexterity saving throw. On a failure, they take 7 (2d6) bludgeoning damage and are knocked prone. On a success, they take half damage.
The rear hatch unseals and opens.
The rear hatch closes and seals.

item.name: Arcfire Glaive
entries: This ever-burning weapon can be spun in whirling attacks, threatening everyone around the wielder. You gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. When you hit with it, the target takes an additional 2d8 fire damage.

As an action, you can use the glaive to make a whirling attack in a 10-foot radius. Creatures within the radius must make a DC 15 Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, they take 2d8 fire damage. On a success, they take half as much. Any flammable objects in the radius ignite.

item.name: Asp of Perfection
entries: This armlet is fashioned of glittering gemweave, woven in the shape of a coiled serpent with bared fangs. Originally worn by the Air Empress, the Asp enables utter perfection, but punishes weakness with death. While you are attuned to the Asp, any attack roll, ability check, or saving throw other than a 1 is treated as a natural 20.

If you roll a 1 on an ability check, attack roll, or saving throw while attuned to the Asp, you drop to 0 hit points, automatically fail two death saving throws, and have disadvantage on your next death saving throw, which is not converted to a natural 20 by the Asp.

Curse. The Asp of Perfection is cursed, and becoming attuned to it extends the curse to you. While cursed, a creature may not willingly remove the Asp. If it is forcibly removed, the cursed creature dies.

item.name: Aspecter's Flute
entries: This slender blowgun shoots tiny needles infused with batface venom. When you hit with the Aspecter's Flute, the target must make a DC 15 Wisdom saving throw. On a failure, they take 1d8 poison damage of which they are unaware. On a success, they take half as much and are aware of the damage.

item.name: Basin of Dreaming
entries: This large stone basin has 5 charges and regains 1d3 expended charges daily at dawn. The basin is about 1 foot in diameter and half as deep. It weighs 10 pounds and holds about 3 gallons. While the basin is filled with water, you can use an action to expend one of its charges to cast the dream spell.
You can also use all 5 charges to cast planeshift.

item.name: Bead of Breeze
entries: This fragile glass bead is chilly to the touch. You can use a bonus action to crush the bead, releasing a cool breeze that can be felt by creatures in a 50-foot sphere centered on you. The breeze lasts for 1 minute.

item.name: Bead of Frost
entries: This small icy bead is kept in a specially-prepared pouch until ready to be used. You can use an action to touch the bead to an object no larger than a 5-foot cube. The object becomes covered in a thin layer of magical frost. If the object was on fire, the fire is extinguished, and the object is chilled. The bead melts after 6 seconds, after which the object's temperature returns to normal.

item.name: Blood Siphon
entries: This pair of small ruby discs creates a magical link between two creatures, drawing the life-force from one creature to another in a parasitic bond. One disc has an engraving of a leech, and the other has an engraving of a flower. As an action, you can place the leech disc on the skin of a humanoid creature. That creature must make a DC 16 Constitution saving throw. On a failure, the leech disc attaches and cannot be removed except by magic such as a remove curse spell. The flower disc can be attached to the skin of another creature as a bonus action, and removed as a bonus action.

At the time that both discs are attached to living creatures, the creature wearing the flower disc can choose one of the following abilities to siphon: Strength, Dexterity, Constitution, or Charisma. The ability cannot be changed unless both discs are removed and re-attached. The creature wearing the flower disc has advantage on ability checks, attack rolls, and saving throws using the chosen ability. The creature wearing the leech disc has disadvantage with the same ability.
Every 24 hours, starting when both discs are attached, the creature wearing the leech disc must make a DC 16 Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, they take one level of exhaustion.

item.name: Blinding Weapon
entries: The first time you hit a creature with this weapon, they must make a DC 13 Constitution saving throw or be blinded until the end of their turn.

item.name: Brightshatter Jar
entries: This sealed, spherical jar, when broken, creates a magical light for 1 hour. The light is centered on the place the jar was broken, casting bright light in a 50-foot radius and dim light for an additional 50 feet.

If the jar is broken in darkness, creatures within the bright light must make a DC 10 Constitution saving throw. On a failure, they have disadvantage on attack rolls and Perception checks relying on sight until the end of their next turn.

item.name: Cord of Ethereal Projection
entries: This rope, woven from the fibers of magical seaweed, is 100 feet long, has 20 hit points, and can be burst with a DC 25 Strength check.

While touching the rope, you can use an action to cast etherealness on yourself. The spell creates an ethereal projection of the rope. You are unable to move the ethereal rope, which occupies the same space as the original rope. The spell only lasts as long as you are touching the ethereal rope, and ends if the original rope or its ethereal projection are severed.
Only one creature can use the rope in this way at a time. If a second creature uses an action to cast the spell while touching the rope, the spell ends for the first creature.

item.name: Cloak of the Beekeeper
entries: While attuned to this cloak, you have resistance to poison damage and advantage on saving throws against being poisoned by the stings of bees, wasps, and other insects.

item.name: Crafting Hoop
entries: This magical hoop of twigs or woven grasses offers a simple enchantment which allows a closer look at small objects. Its grants advantage on any ability check made to appraise or inspect an item that is small or highly detailed.

item.name: Day-Star Pendant
entries: These ivory pendants are given to members of the Council of Day (p. 193). Each pendant is inset with four tiny stones in the pattern of a diamond. When these stones are pulled into the center, they glow brightly, emitting a dim light in a 10 foot radius.

item.name: Deepstone
entries: Also called the Mountainheart, this legendary item was the prize of the Stone Empire. A large, egg-shaped stone of strange, rough texture, the Deepstone grants powerful control over the element of earth. When touching the Deepstone, you can cast the following spells at will with a range of 1 mile: (at-will) mold earth, move earth, erupting earth, stone shape, transmute rock, and (once per day) earthquake.

Additionally, while you are attuned to the Deepstone, you gain the benefits of the investiture of stone spell, and can cast clairvoyance once per day, locating its sensor within 1 mile of the Deepstone.

item.name: Direstaff
entries: This quarterstaff bears a minor enchantment. As a bonus action, you can double the length of the staff up to 10 feet, granting it the Reach property. When doubled in length, attacks made with a direstaff have disadvantage. You can return the direstaff to its original length with a bonus action.

item.name: Dowsing Rod
entries: This enchanted forked stick is sensitive to the proximity of hidden water. While walking and holding the stick, you are aware if you are moving closer to or further from potable water.

item.name: Duru's Cudgel
item.reqAttune: by a druid, warlock, or guardian
