entries: This enormous greatclub is a legendary bough from the original tree that was Duru himself. You gain a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. If a creature makes an attack with this greatclub without being attuned to it, they take 4d6 necrotic damage as tiny leaves and flowers burst through their skin from the inside.

While you are attuned to Duru's Cudgel, creatures you hit with this weapon must make a DC 18 Wisdom saving throw. A creature that fails the save begins to turn into a tree and is restrained. The restrained creature must repeat the saving throw at the end of its next turn, transforming into a tree on a failure or ending the effect on a success. The transformation lasts until the creature is freed by greater restoration or other magic. A creature that fails the initial saving throw by 5 or more instantly transforms into a tree.

item.name: Embracing Net
entries: This magical net is designed to squeeze the life out of its victims. You can use a bonus action to magically constrict it, dealing 1d6 bludgeoning damage to a creature restrained by it. If the net is destroyed by slashing damage, it weaves itself back together at the next dawn.

item.name: Eyes of the Storm
entries: These paired sapphires are attached by an ivory chain or cord of finely woven seaweed. While holding the Eyes of the Storm, your vision is not obscured by rain, snow, fog, or other weather effects. As an action, you can throw the Eyes up to 30 feet into the air. They hover at the apex of your throw for up to 1 minute. While they remain in the air, you can use your action to see through them until the end of your next turn. You are blind in regards to your own sense of sight while using the Eyes in this way. You can use your action on subsequent turns to maintain your vision through the Eyes until the end of your next turn.

item.name: Firehide
entries: This heavy fur cloak, if ignited, burns perpetually with intangible fire until deprived of air or submerged in water.

item.name: Fluttering Mail
entries: This gauzy armor is enchanted with a permanent illusion to look and feel like a gleaming, loosely-woven garment, while still offering its wearer protection. You can don or doff this armor as an action.

item.name: Gnomeknack Crossbow
entries: This ingenious device is three weapons in one. As a bonus action, you can speak the command word and transform this weapon into any of its three forms: a hand crossbow, a light crossbow, or a heavy crossbow.

item.name: Gutter's Knife
entries: This knife is ideal for harvesting a kill, and reduces the time spent when making Wisdom (Medicine or Survival) checks to harvest by half.

item.name: Icebiter Pike
entries: This frost giant pike was a prized weapon of the Stone Empire. While attuned to the Icebiter Pike, you gain a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon, and deal an additional 1d8 cold damage.
As an action, you can use Icebiter Pike to cast cone of cold with a save DC of 14. Once used, the cone of cold property can't be used again for 24 hours.

item.name: Knotpick
entries: This enchanted bone tool carries subtle enchantments to help ease apart magically secured locks and knots. When making checks with thieves tools, the knotpick noiselessly suppresses arcane lock as if with the knock spell, after which the knotpick breaks.

item.name: Krakenhunt Trident
entries: This trident can create a spectral echo of itself, allowing its wielder to both throw it and stab with it. While attuned to this weapon, you can use a bonus action to create a magical duplicate of the weapon in your hand. The duplicate feels identical to the original trident, but has a shimmering, translucent quality. The duplicate disappears after you make an attack with it. You can only have one such duplicate at any time.

item.name: Lance of the Lodge
entries: This blood-red lance is the ultimate prize for the Redguard of Edgegather, famed for its ability to summon an entire hunting party out of thin air. The lance has 10 charges. While attuned to the lance of the lodge, you can expend 1 charge and use an action to cast find steed. The summoned steed appears underneath you in red bone armor, granting +1 to its AC, and you are automatically riding it.

Also, while attuned to the lance, you can expend 2 charges and use an action to cast conjure animals at 3rd level. You cannot use the lance to cast this spell again until all beasts previously conjured by this spell have dropped to 0 hit points or disappeared.

Finally, while attuned to the lance, you can cast find familiar. You can only summon creatures with a fly speed using this spell, and the range for telepathy with your familiar is extended to 500 feet.

item.name: Lightning Branch
entries: This enchanted branch was prepared by a spellcaster and then struck by lightning, which it captured in its wood. When you hit with this club, it deals an additional 1d6 lightning damage.

item.name: Little Liar
entries: This intricately designed knife brims with illusion magic. Little Liar has 6 charges. When you hit with it, you can expend charges to cast one of the following spells without expending a spell slot (spell save DC 14), centered on the target:

items: phantasmal force (2 charges)
fear (3 charges)
phantasmal killer (4 charges)

entries: Little Liar regains 1d4 + 2 expended charges daily at dawn.

item.name: Mask of Expression
entries: This carved mask, made of wood, ivory, or stone, shows the expressions chosen by its wearer. The wearer can change the expression shown at will.

item.name: Mask of the Arbiter
entries: The Arbiter of Seerfall traditionally wears this unadorned wooden mask. Within a 30-foot radius of the mask, creatures are affected by the zone of truth spell as if they had failed the save.
While attuned to the mask, the wearer has truesight up to 30 feet.

item.name: Marble of Nod
entries: This translucent purple stone is smooth and cool to the touch. As a bonus action, you can place it in your mouth. When you do so, you instantly fall asleep and begin to dream. Creatures that cannot sleep are unaffected.

item.name: Midnight Star
entries: This legendary storm giant weapon was wielded by none other than the Sea Emperor himself. When you attune to this weapon, you and up to 6 creatures you choose within line of sight gain truesight up to 60 feet as long as you are attuned to Midnight Star. You gain a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon while attuned to it. When you hit with Midnight Star, it deals an additional 2d8 thunder damage.
Additionally, as an action, while attuned to Midnight Star you can cast foresight without expending a spell slot. This property can't be used again for 24 hours.

item.name: Monstertooth Club
entries: This club is adorned with the teeth of monsters. When you hit with this club, it deals an additional 1d4 damage. The DM chooses the type or determines it randomly from the table below:

entries.caption: Damage Type
rows: Acid (ooze, beast)
Necrotic (undead, fiend)
Cold (elemental)
Poison (beast, plant)
Fire (dragon, fiend)
Psychic (aberration, fey)
Lightning (beast, fey)
Radiant (celestial)

item.name: Mindfang
entries: This black wooden ring is carved in the shape of a snake, its fangs poised over its wearer's finger. While attuned to Mindfang, you can use an action to cast detect thoughts without expending a spell slot (spell save DC 20), taking 1d4 psychic damage as Mindfang sinks its fangs into your finger. Each time you use an action or a bonus action using the spell, you take psychic damage, with the size of the die increasing by one size to 1d6, then 1d8, 1d10, and finally reaching its maximum at 1d12. The die size resets when you take a short or long rest.
While using Mindfang, you can continue to push into a target's mind, repeatedly probing deeper as an action without ending the spell.

Cursed. Mindfang is cursed and cannot be removed once worn except by magic such as the remove curse spell. It speaks telepathically to the wearer, urging them to use it and goading them to go deeper into their target's mind, promising valuable information from its victims if they probe a little more.

item.name: Obsidian Armor
entries: You have a +1 bonus to your AC while wearing this armor. Additionally, you gain resistance to fire damage.

item.name: Peakshatter Gauntlets
entries: While wearing these gauntlets, you gain a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls of unarmed strikes made with these gauntlets. When you hit with these gauntlets, you deal an additional 1d4 thunder damage. Any damage you deal to objects or structures with this unarmed strike is doubled.
Additionally, if you damage an object or structure using an unarmed strike with these gauntlets, you can use your bonus action to make another unarmed strike against the same target.

item.name: Pipe of Smoke-Summoning
entries: This black pipe has a long, twisted stem. As an action, you can smoke the pipe to cast conjure minor elementals at 4th level without expending a spell slot. Summoned elementals must be at least partly comprised of fire or air. You can't use this feature again until the next dawn.

If you smoke this pipe using costly supplies, you can use its spellcasting feature at higher levels. When you smoke this pipe using materials worth 500 ps, which the pipe consumes, you can cast the spell at 6th level. You can cast it at 8th level with materials worth 1,000 ps, which it consumes.

item.name: Platter of Endless Delight
entries: This elegant wooden platter is carved in the pattern of a blooming flower, and offers a limitless bounty of delicious food, such as fruits and vegetables, meats and cheeses, nuts and berries, or more complex offerings. As an action, you can change the type of food the platter presents. The food looks, feels, and tastes real, but offers no physical benefit, and you cannot satisfy your hunger by eating from it.

item.name: Ring of Understanding
entries: This white coral ring gives the gift of deeper knowledge of secret matters. While attuned to this ring, you have advantage on Intelligence (Arcana, History, and Nature) checks, and can understand any language that you hear spoken for at least 10 minutes.

item.name: Rockturner
entries: This diamond-shaped tool bears an old enchantment from deep within the Quakewaste. As an action, you can touch it to a stone object of Medium size or smaller, or a section of stone no more than 5 feet in any dimension, and command it to form into any shape that suits your purpose, such as a doorway, weapon, or container. The shape cannot have fine mechanical detail.

Over the course of 24 hours, as long as the Rockturner is touching the stone, the stone slowly shifts into your commanded shape. If the Rockturner ceases touching the stone, the effect ends, and the stone remains in the shape it was when it lost contact with the Rockturner.

item.name: Remorhaz Armor
entries: This chitinous armor is made from the plates and spines of the remorhaz. While wearing this armor, you have resistance to cold damage. When a creature touches you or hits you with an unarmed strike or natural weapon, you can use your reaction to sear the creature with the innate heat of the remorhaz's plates, dealing 1d6 fire damage.

item.name: Rumbleglow Paint
entries: Typically found in a clay pot (weighing 1 pound in total), this drab paint has a gooey consistency and a gray-brown color that blends in with most rocks, wood, and earth. Each pot of paint is sufficient to cover 1,000 square feet of a surface, and it takes 10 minutes to cover 100 square feet with paint.

Once dry, the paint glows in response to vibration. Faint sounds and tremors cause it to shed dim light for 5 feet, with louder sounds or stronger tremors causing the light to glow brighter. The color of the light alters with the pitch of the vibration, with low-pitched sounds causing cool-colored light such as blues, purples, and greens, and high-pitched sounds causing warm-colored light such as reds, oranges, and yellows, with shrill noises causing the paint to glow white.

item.name: Scimitar of the Dancer
entries: This elegant weapon grants its wielder preternatural grace. While attuned to the Scimitar of the Dancer, you have advantage on Dexterity saving throws, Dexterity (Acrobatics) checks and Charisma (Performance) checks to dance.

item.name: Scouting Hoop
entries: This magical hoop of small bones and leather straps offers a simple enchantment. Objects and creatures viewed through this hoop are magnified to twice their size.

item.name: Nightwatch Scouting Hoop
entries: Objects and creatures viewed through this enchanted hoop are magnified to twice their size. Additionally, when looking through this hoop, you can see with darkvision.

item.name: Shield-Axe of Ush
entries: This heavy bone shield is formed of the skull of a powerful dinosaur, and marked with divine enchantment. While wearing the shield, you can use a bonus action to strike it, causing it to transform into a bone greataxe with a brief burst of ivory-colored smoke. You can also use a bonus action to strike the greataxe, transforming it back into a shield, which you instantly don without using an action.

item.name: Sickle of Sacrifice
entries: This necromantic blade is a tool of dark spellskins, and features strange marks and carvings along its curved blade. If you target a humanoid who has at least one hand for an attack with this sickle and roll a critical hit, in addition to dealing damage, you sever one of the target's hands of your choosing. The severed hand immediately reanimates as a Crawling Claw, which acts on your initiative and obeys your commands.
If the hand was holding an item or bore a magic ring that the target was attuned to, the item is dropped and the attunement is broken.

item.name: Speaker's Tongue
entries: This small chalk token is the size and shape of a human tongue. Crushing the token activates a stored enchantment in the chalk. For one minute, you can be understood by all creatures who know at least one language, as if you were speaking in the language with which they are most familiar. You hear yourself speaking in your own language.

item.name: Starcouncil Diadem
entries: This gleaming circlet is fashioned of glittering gems inset on an ivory lattice. While wearing the Starcouncil Diadem, you can cast contact other plane without expending a spell slot. You cannot use the diadem to cast this spell again until the next dawn.

item.name: Stonearrow
entries: This terrible arrow of the Stone Empire is feared by all, transforming into a hurtling boulder in mid-flight. When you use a bow to hit on a ranged attack with this arrow, deal 4d10 bludgeoning damage. The target must make a DC 17 Strength saving throw or be knocked prone. Creatures within a 10-foot radius of the target must make a DC 10 Strength saving throw. On a failure, they take the same damage and are knocked prone. On a success, they take half damage.

item.name: Stonebow
entries: This enchanted bow fires arrows that transform into large stones in midair. When you hit on a ranged attack with this bow, the target takes 1d10 bludgeoning damage and must succeed on a DC 11 Strength saving throw or be knocked prone.

item.name: Sunglobe
entries: Upon uttering a command word, this small ivory sphere will levitate 6 feet into the air and emanate light similar to a daylight spell for 1 hour. Unlike a normal daylight spell, the light from the sunglobe is equivalent to sunlight. If the sunglobe is moved, it both the light and levitation effects immediately end.
Once it has been used, the sunglobe cannot be used again until the next dawn.

These orbs are often used by cave-dwelling or dungeon-exploring dreas who require daily sunlight for their photosynthesis. They can also be effective against vampires and other creatures sensitive to sunlight.

item.name: Threehorn Halberd
entries: This halberd was made by giants from the sliced skull of a triceratops. You gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. When you deal damage using this halberd, you can release a bellow from the bone that targets the damaged creature. The target must make a DC 13 Constitution saving throw or be incapacitated until the start of its next turn.
Additionally, you can use the halberd to parry. As a reaction, when you would take damage from a melee attack, you can increase your AC by 1.

item.name: Victory's Cost
entries: This charred crown of bone and fang commands the attention of those viewing it, and seems to burn with an inner flame. When summoned, the living flame from the crown forms a writhing blade of immolation that hungrily burns at any that draw near. While attuned to this item, you can use a bonus action to speak a command word and cause a living flame that functions as a magical longsword to appear in your hands. You are considered proficient with the sword, and gain a +3 to attack and damage rolls made with this weapon. It has the finesse and light properties, and deals fire damage instead of slashing damage. You can dismiss the sword with a bonus action.

Whenever you use the sword to hit a creature or a flammable object, it ignites. Until a creature takes an action to douse the fire, the target takes 1d10 fire damage at the start of each of its turns.

While the blade persists, you are the center of an immolation that burns those around you. When a creature starts their turn within 10 feet of you, or enters that space for the first time on a turn, they take 2d6 fire damage. You are affected by this aura.
The fire damage caused by this sword ignores resistance to fire damage, and treats immunity to fire damage as resistance.

Curse. This crown is cursed, and becoming attuned to it extends the curse to you. Until the curse is broken with remove curse or similar magic, you are unwilling to part with the crown, keeping it within reach at all times. In addition, the flaming sword demands to be fed. Once summoned, it cannot be dismissed until damage dealt by the sword brings a creature to 0 hit points.

item.name: Wand of Radiance
entries: This short wand is made of a smooth material like the shell of a turtle. The wand has 6 charges. As an action, you can spend a charge to make a ranged spell attack against a target you can see within 120 feet. Before you make an attack with this wand, you can determine whether or not any damage dealt is nonlethal. On a hit, deal 2d10 radiant damage. If you roll a 1 on an attack roll with the wand, it emits smoke and bright sparks and cannot be used again until repaired with magic such as mending.
The wand regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

item.name: Well of Entrapment
entries: This large enchanted stone is a flat square 5 feet across and 6 inches thick, which weighs 200 pounds. You can use an action to place the well of entrapment flat on the ground, whereupon the well of entrapment creates an extradimensional hole 15 feet deep. The space within the hole exists on a different plane, so it can't be used to open passages. The stone can be lifted to reveal the hole, which remains as long as the stone keeps at least one edge or corner touching the ground. If the stone loses contact with the ground, all creatures or objects within the hole remain in the extradimensional hole with no exit, and begin to suffocate within 10 minutes.

The floor of the hole is covered in a half inch of cold saltwater, and the sides of the hole are covered in slick spikes made of shell fragments. A creature attempting to climb must make a DC 18 Dexterity or Strength (Athletics) check. On a failure, they take 1d6 slashing damage and fall prone. On a success, they take half damage.
The top of the hole is magically weighted, and cannot be opened from the inside without a DC 25 Strength check or magic capable of lifting 2,000 pounds.

item.name: Whip of Bosa
entries: This efreeti weapon was once lost in the ruined giant city of Bosa. The long black thong of the whip crackles with flame. You have a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this weapon, which has a reach of 30 feet and deals 4d6 fire damage on a hit. When you hit a creature with the whip of Bosa, if another creature is within 30 feet of the target, you may make an attack roll against them, using the whip. If that attack hits, you may target another creature within 30 feet, and so on, continuing until you miss a target. You cannot target the same creature more than once in a single attack with the whip of Bosa.

item.name: Whispershell
entries: This smooth green shell was once the home of a fey sea creature, and can be used to leave messages. When someone puts their mouth to the shell's opening, any message they speak up to 1 minute in length will be remembered by the shell. When they or anyone else holds the shell to their ear, they can hear the last message spoken into it.

item.name: Wolfskull Hood
entries: This hood, fashioned from the enchanted skull of a wolf, was worn by the Pack, a secretive band of giant-hunters from the Fields of Fargone. While wearing this hood, you have advantage on an attack roll against a creature if at least one of your allies is within 5 feet of the creature and the ally isn't incapacitated. Additionally, you have advantage on Wisdom (Perception) checks that rely on hearing or smell.

item.name: Writhing Flail
entries: This flail was originally recovered from a vault of aberrations, and features 8 short rubbery tendrils with hooks and barbs at the end. The tendrils writhe slightly when the flail is touched.

When you hit a creature with the writhing flail, instead of doing damage, you can choose to attempt to grapple the creature using the flail's Dexterity bonus, which is +1. If the flail successfully grapples the creature, it wraps its tendrils around the creature's head. The creature is blinded and cannot breathe while grappled by the flail. While grappling a creature in this way, the writhing flail cannot be used to attack another creature.

If the flail has a creature grappled at the start of your turn, it will attempt to extract the creature's brain. The creature must make a DC 13 Constitution saving throw or take 2d10 piercing damage. If this damage reduces the creature's health to 0, the flail extracts the creature's brain (if any), and releases the grapple.

monster.name: Glelh the Unblinking

trait.name: Spiritual Form.
entries: When Glelh would be reduced to 0 hit points, he is instead reduced to 1 hit point. He gains a number of temporary hit points equal to half of his maximum hit points and ends all conditions affecting him. He then takes on his Spiritual Form, and his features and actions change accordingly.

trait.name: Keen Senses.
entries: Glelh has advantage on Wisdom (Perception) checks.

trait.name: Running Leap.
entries: With a 10-foot start, Glelh can long jump up to 50 feet.

trait.name: Pounce.
entries: If Glelh moves at least 20 feet straight toward a creature and then hits it with a claw attack on the same turn, that target must succeed on a DC 23 Strength saving throw or be knocked prone. If the target is prone, Glelh can make one bite attack against it as a bonus action.

trait.name: Unblinking Gaze.
entries: As a bonus action, Glelh chooses one creature within 60 feet that he can see and subjects it to one of the following gazes:

items.name: 1. Fiery Gaze.
items.entry: The creature must make a DC 20 Dexterity saving throw, taking 35 (10d6) radiant or fire damage on a failure, or half as much on a success (Glelh's choice).

items.name: 2. Blinding Gaze.
items.entry: The creature must make a DC 20 Constitution saving throw, becoming blinded for 1 minute on a failure.

items.name: 3. Paralyzing Gaze.
items.entry: The creature must make a DC 20 Wisdom saving throw, becoming paralyzed until the end of Glelh's next turn on a failure.

items.name: 4. Shunting Gaze.
items.entry: The creature must make a DC 20 Charisma saving throw. On a failure, the creature is magically teleported to an unoccupied space within 60 feet of Glelh.

items.name: 5. Hypnotic Gaze.
items.entry: The creature must make a DC 20 Wisdom saving throw, becoming charmed for 1 minute on a failure. While charmed, the creature is incapacitated and must spend its movement on each of its turns moving towards Glelh in the most direct manner possible, making no attempts to avoid attacks of opportunity. When the creature takes damage from a source other than Glelh, the target can repeat the Wisdom saving throw, ending the charm effect on a success.

trait.name: Innate Spellcasting.
entries: Glelh's spellcasting ability is Charisma (spell save DC 20). Glelh can innately cast any spell from the guardian (paladin) or shaman (cleric) spell list, requiring no material components, and has the following spells prepared:
At-will: aid, command, cure wounds, detect evil and good, detect magic, guidance, guiding bolt, healing word, hold person, lesser restoration, sacred flame (4d8), silence
6/day: banishment, daylight, dispel evil and good, flamestrike, greater restoration, hallow, heal, mass healing word, remove curse
3/day: control weather, divine word, firestorm, holy aura, planeshift, regenerate
1/day: gate, foresight, mass heal

trait.name: Magic Resistance.
entries: Glelh has advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (3/day).
entries: If Glelh fails a saving throw, he can choose to succeed instead.

action.name: Multiattack.
entries: Glelh uses unblinking gaze and makes three attacks: two with his claws and one with his bite.

action.name: Claw.
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +15 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 22 (4d6 + 8) slashing damage.

action.name: Bite.
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +15 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 30 (4d10 + 8) piercing damage and the target is grappled (escape DC 19).

action.name: Hunt God's Fury (Recharges 5–6).
entries: Glelh makes six claw attacks.

action.name: Gather Magic (2/Day).
entries: Glelh focuses on his connection to the world's magic. If he has cast any spells with a per-day limit, the limit is reset as if he had not cast any such spells yet.

legendary.name: Claw.
entries: Glelh makes a claw attack.

legendary.name: Maneuver.
entries: Glelh moves up to half his speed without provoking attacks of opportunity.

legendary.name: Gaze (Costs 2 Actions).
entries: Glelh uses Unblinking Gaze.

legendary.name: Lunging Bite (Costs 2 Actions).
entries: Glelh moves up to half his speed and makes a bite attack.

legendaryHeader: Glelh can take 3 legendary actions, choosing from the options below. Only one legendary action option can be used at a time and only at the end of another creature's turn. Glelh regains spent legendary actions at the start of his turn.

monster.name: Glelh, Spiritual Form

trait.name: Spiritual Form.
entries: When Glelh takes on his spiritual form, he retains his hit dice and hit point maximum from his previous form. Features with limited uses that are present across multiple forms, such as Legendary Resistance, do not regain spent uses. While in his Spiritual Form, Glelh cannot take lair actions.

trait.name: Shattered Silence.
entries: When Glelh enters his spiritual form, he uses Resonating Roar and his physical body is consumed by inner light, leaving behind a silhouette of divine energy. All magical darkness in Glelh's hollow is dispelled, and all creatures within 120 feet of Glelh must make a DC 20 Constitution saving throw or be blinded until the end of their next turn. Glelh can maintain this form for 1 minute, after which he returns to his physical form, loses his temporary hit points, and takes 5 points of exhaustion, which can only be removed by resting or a wish spell.

trait.name: Avatar of Light.
entries: Glelh sheds bright light in a 50-foot radius and dim light for an additional 50 feet. When a creature starts their turn within 10 feet of Glelh or comes within 10 feet of Glelh for the first time on a turn, they must make a DC 20 Constitution saving throw or become blinded until the start of their next turn.

trait.name: Radiant Form.
entries: When a creature touches Glelh or hits him with a melee attack while within 10 feet of him, they take 14 (4d6) radiant damage. Whenever Glelh would take radiant damage, he instead takes no damage and regains hit points equal to the radiant damage dealt.

trait.name: Magic Resistance.
entries: Glelh has advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (3/day).
entries: If Glelh fails a saving throw, he can choose to succeed instead.

action.name: Multiattack.
entries: Glelh makes two searing touch attacks and uses either Sunglimpse or Resonating Roar, if available.

action.name: Searing Touch.
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +15 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 32 (7d6 + 8) radiant or fire damage (Glelh's choice).

action.name: Sunglimpse (Recharge 5-6).
entries: Burning light erupts in a 30-foot sphere centered on a point Glelh can see within 60 feet of him. All creatures in the area must make a DC 21 Dexterity saving throw, taking 42 (12d6) radiant damage on a failure, or half as much on a success. Additionally, creatures that fail their saving throw are blinded until the end of Glelh's next turn.

action.name: Resonating Roar (Recharge 5-6).
entries: Glelh releases a roar laced with divine energy. Each time he roars before his Spiritual Form ends, the roar evolves, as shown below. Once Glelh has used his final roar, he cannot roar for the remainder of his spiritual form.

items.name: 1. First Roar.
items.entry: Glelh gains resistance to damage until the start of his next turn, and all other creatures within 300 feet must make a DC 21 Wisdom saving throw, becoming incapacitated until the end of Glelh's next turn on a failure.

items.name: 2. Second Roar.
items.entry: All other creatures in Glelh's hallow must make a DC 21 Strength saving throw. On a failure, a creature is knocked prone and their movement speed is reduced to 0 for 1 minute. At the end of a prone creature's turn, they can repeat the saving throw, standing up and regaining their movement speed on a success.

items.name: 3. Third Roar.
items.entry: All other creatures in Glelh's hallow must make a DC 21 Constitution saving throw, becoming stunned for 1 minute on a failure. A stunned creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of their turns, ending the effect on a success.

items.name: 4. Final Roar.
items.entry: All other creatures in Glelh's hallow must make a DC 21 Wisdom saving throw, taking 55 (10d10) force damage on a failure, or half as much on a success. If a creature that failed their saving throw has 100 hit points or less, they instead die.

legendary.name: Searing Touch.
entries: Glelh makes a searing touch attack.

legendary.name: Unsight (Costs 2 Actions).
entries: All blinded creatures within 60 feet of Glelh must make a DC 21 Constitution saving throw, becoming blinded for an additional 1 minute on a failure.

legendary.name: Roar (Costs 3 Actions).
entries: Glelh uses Resonating Roar.

legendaryHeader: Glelh can take 3 legendary actions, choosing from the options below. Only one legendary action option can be used at a time and only at the end of another creature's turn. Glelh regains spent legendary actions at the start of his turn.

monster.name: Mala Long-Song

trait.name: Spiritual Form.
entries: When Mala would be reduced to 0 hit points, she is instead reduced to 1 hit point. She gains a number of temporary hit points equal to half of her maximum hit points and ends all conditions affecting her. She then takes on her Spiritual Form, and her features and actions change accordingly.

trait.name: Reflective Hide.
entries: Any time Mala is targeted by a magic missile spell, a line spell, or a spell that requires a ranged attack roll, roll a d6. On a 1 to 5, Mala is unaffected. On a 6, Mala is unaffected, and the effect is reflected back at the caster as though it originated from Mala, turning the caster into the target.

trait.name: Magic Resistance.
entries: Mala has advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

trait.name: Innate Spellcasting.
entries: Mala's spellcasting ability is Charisma (spell save DC 25). Mala can innately cast any spell from any spell list, requiring no material components, and has the following spells prepared:
At-will: aid, bane, bless, command, cure wounds, detect evil and good, detect magic, faerie fire, fog cloud, healing word, hold person, lesser restoration, light, sacred flame (4d8), spare the dying
9/day: arcane eye, banishment, bestow curse, blade barrier, call lightning, daylight, death ward, dispel magic, greater restoration, harm, heal, speak with dead
6/day: divine word, feeblemind, forcecage, holy aura, planeshift, regenerate, sunburst, symbol, teleport
3/day: foresight, gate, imprisonment, power word kill, timestop, wish

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (3/Day).
entries: If Mala fails a saving throw, she can choose to succeed instead.

action.name: Multiattack.
entries: Mala uses Deepsong. She then makes two attacks: one with her tail and one with her Divine Gaze. Mala can use Prison Realm in place of her Divine Gaze.

action.name: Tail.
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +19 to hit, reach 20 ft., up to three targets that are not grappled by Mala. Hit: 28 (4d8 + 10) bludgeoning damage. This damage is magical.

action.name: Divine Gaze.
entries: Ranged Spell Attack: +17 to hit, range 120 ft., one target. Hit: 29 (6d6 + 8) radiant damage, and the target must make a DC 24 Constitution saving throw or be stunned until the end of their next turn.

action.name: Prison Realm.
entries: Mala makes a Divine Gaze attack against a stunned, restrained, paralyzed, or blinded creature. If the attack hits, the target takes the Divine Gaze's damage and must make a DC 24 Charisma saving throw or be affected as by the maze spell.

action.name: Deepsong (6/day).
entries: Mala sings a divine note that reverberates with magic. Mala chooses one of the following effects:

items.name: 1. Song of Creation's Fire.
items.entry: Creatures of Mala's choice within 120 feet must make a DC 24 Dexterity saving throw, taking 33 (6d10) fire damage on a failure, or half as much on a success.

items.name: 2. Song of the Captive.
items.entry: Creatures of Mala's choice within 120 feet must make a DC 24 Strength saving throw or become restrained for 1 minute. A restrained creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of their turns, ending the restrained effect on a success.

items.name: 3. Song of the Starry Traveler.
items.entry: Creatures of Mala's choice within 120 feet must make a DC 24 Charisma saving throw or be transported to a random location as if by the plane shift spell. The plane of existence to which they are sent is randomly determined by the DM.

entries: Mala cannot use the same song two times in a row.

action.name: Crushing Vortex (Recharge 5-6).
entries: Mala creates a swirling vortex in a 60-foot sphere centered on a point Mala can see within 120 feet. Creatures in the area must make a DC 24 Strength saving throw, taking 44 (8d10) bludgeoning damage and being dragged 30 feet towards the center of the vortex on a failure, or taking half as much damage and being dragged only 15 feet on a success.

legendary.name: Swim.
entries: Mala moves up to her swim speed without provoking attacks of opportunity.

legendary.name: Tail.
entries: Mala makes a tail attack.

legendary.name: Deepsong (Costs 2 Actions).
entries: Mala uses Deepsong.

legendary.name: Show of Power (Costs 3 Actions).
entries: Mala uses one of her rechargeable abilities, if available.

legendary.name: Cast a Spell (Costs 3 Actions).
entries: Mala casts a spell from her list of prepared spells, consuming a spell slot as normal.

legendaryHeader: Mala can take 3 legendary actions, choosing from the options below. Only one legendary action option can be used at a time and only at the end of another creature's turn. Mala regains spent legendary actions at the start of her turn.

monster.name: Mala, Spiritual Form

trait.name: Spiritual Form.
entries: When Mala takes on her spiritual form, she retains her hit dice and hit point maximum from her previous form. Features with limited uses that are present across multiple forms, such as Legendary Resistance, do not regain spent uses. While in her Spiritual Form, Mala cannot take Lair Actions.

trait.name: The Keeper's Heart.
entries: When Mala's spiritual form feature activates, Mala's physical form swells even as it dissipates, and all creatures in Mala's hollow are drawn into the realm of Mala's spiritual heart; an ethereal place where darkness meets light and life meets death. What remains of Mala is her spiritual heart, an orb of swirling black-and-white essence. While within Mala's heart realm, creatures are always within range of Mala's attacks, spells, and magical abilities. Mala can maintain this form for 1 minute, after which she returns to her physical form, loses her temporary hit points, and takes 5 points of exhaustion, which can only be removed by resting or a wish spell.

trait.name: Presence of the First Keeper.
entries: Creatures that start their turn within 30 feet of Mala must make a DC 25 Constitution saving throw, taking 10 (3d6) radiant or necrotic damage (Mala chooses) on a failure, or half as much on a success. Creatures that fail their saving throw are also deafened and unable to speak until the start of their next turn.

trait.name: Magic Resistance.
entries: Mala has advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

trait.name: Magic Weapons.
entries: Mala's weapon attacks are magical.

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (3/Day).
entries: If Mala fails a saving throw, she can choose to succeed instead.

action.name: Multiattack.
entries: Mala uses Terrible Revelation and Extinguish Soul. She then makes two attacks: one with Touch of Life and one with Touch of Death.

action.name: Touch of Death.
entries: Melee Spell Attack: +17 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 29 (6d6 + 8) necrotic damage.

action.name: Touch of Life.
entries: Melee Spell Attack: +17 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 29 (6d6 + 8) radiant damage.

action.name: Extinguish Soul.
entries: Mala chooses a creature that she can see within 120 feet. That creature must make a DC 24 Constitution saving throw, taking 44 (8d10) necrotic damage on a failure. If this damage reduces the creature to 0 hit points, the creature dies, and cannot be returned to life by any means short of a wish spell until Mala dies.

action.name: Terrible Revelation.
entries: Mala shares a piece of terrible knowledge with a creature of her choice that she can see within 120 feet. The creature must make a DC 24 Intelligence saving throw, taking 28 (8d6) psychic damage on a failure, or half as much on a success. A creature that fails its saving throw becomes wracked with a passing madness until the end of its next turn. A maddened creature rolls a d6 to determine what it will do with its turn. On a 1-3, the creature does nothing. On a 4-5, the creature takes no action but spends its movement to move in a random direction, determined by a d8. On a 6, the creature takes the attack action against a random creature, or it does nothing if no creature is within reach.

action.name: Mass Dispel (Recharge 5-6).
entries: All creatures of Mala's choice within her hallow must make a DC 24 Charisma saving throw. When a creature fails their saving throw, any spells affecting the creature end. Additionally, any magical items the creature is carrying or has equipped are suppressed, becoming nonmagical for 1 minute. An affected creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of their turns, ending the magic suppression on a success.

action.name: Baleful Star (Recharge 5-6).
entries: All creatures of Mala's choice within her hollow must make a DC 24 Constitution saving throw, taking 33 (6d10) cold damage and 33 (6d10) radiant damage on a failure, or half as much on a success. Creatures that fail their saving throw by 5 or more are blinded for 1 minute. Blind creatures can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of their turns, ending the blind effect on a success.

legendary.name: Attack.
entries: Mala makes an attack with Touch of Death or Touch of Life.

legendary.name: Extinguish Soul (Costs 2 Actions).
entries: Mala uses Extinguish Soul.

legendary.name: Revelation (Costs 2 Actions).
entries: Mala uses Terrible Revelation.

legendary.name: Unleashed Power (Costs 3 Actions).
entries: Mala uses one of her rechargeable abilities, if available.

legendaryHeader: Mala can take 3 legendary actions, choosing from the options below. Only one legendary action option can be used at a time and only at the end of another creature's turn. Mala regains spent legendary actions at the start of her turn.

monster.name: Kho Many-Arms

trait.name: Spiritual Form.
entries: When Kho would be reduced to 0 hit points, he is instead reduced to 1 hit point. He gains a number of temporary hit points equal to half of his maximum hit points and ends all conditions affecting him. He then takes on his Spiritual Form, and his features and actions change accordingly.

trait.name: Wakeful Rage (1/day).
entries: When Kho starts his turn with half or less of his hit points, he flies into a wakeful rage. While raging, Kho has advantage on ability checks and saving throws that use Strength, he has resistance to piercing, slashing, and bludgeoning damage, and when he makes a weapon attack, he gains a +7 bonus to the damage roll. This rage lasts for 1 minute, until Kho is incapacitated, or until his Spiritual Form feature triggers.

trait.name: Vigilance of the Many-Armed God.
entries: Kho can take four extra reactions per round, which can only be used to make attacks of opportunity with his arms of many or use spirit guard. Additionally, creatures trigger attacks of opportunity from Kho if they move 15 or more feet while within his reach.

trait.name: Innate Spellcasting.
entries: Kho's spellcasting ability is Charisma (spell save DC 23, +15 to hit with spell attacks). Kho can innately cast any spell from the Paladin or Cleric spell list, requiring no material components, and has the following spells prepared:
At-will: blindness/deafness, blink, calm emotions, command, cure wounds, faerie fire, healing word, hold person, mirror image, sleep, silence, remove curse
6/day: arcane eye, banishment, dimension door, dream, geas, hallow, harm, hold monster, modify memory
3/day: control weather, divine word, etherealness, maze, mirage arcane, planeshift
1/day: astral projection, gate, weird

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (3/day).
entries: If Kho fails a saving throw, he can choose to succeed instead.

action.name: Multiattack.
entries: Kho uses Manifest Missile twice or makes four melee attacks, two of which can be fist attacks.

action.name: Fist.
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +17 to hit, reach 15 ft., one creature. Hit: 31 (4d10 + 9) bludgeoning damage. If the target is a creature, Kho can choose to grapple the creature (escape DC 20) instead of dealing damage. While grappled, a medium or smaller creature is restrained. Kho can grapple up to two creatures this way.

action.name: Arm of Many.
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +17 to hit, reach 30 ft., one target. Hit: 23 (4d6 + 9) force damage.

action.name: Manifest Missile.
entries: Kho hurls a missile of spiritual energy to a point that he can see within 120 feet, where it explodes in a 10-foot radius. All creatures in the area must make a DC 23 Dexterity saving throw, taking 28 (8d6) force damage on a failure, or half as much on a success.

action.name: Fearsome Display (Recharge 5-6).
entries: If Kho is in a wakeful rage, he beats his chest and lets out a roar. All creatures within 150 feet of Kho that can see or hear him must make a DC 23 Wisdom saving throw, becoming frightened until the end of Kho's next turn on a failure. While a creature is frightened by this effect, the creature is also paralyzed.

action.name: Gather Magic (2/Day).
entries: Kho focuses on his connection to the world's magic. If he has cast any spells with a per-day limit, the limit is reset as if he had not cast any such spells yet.

reaction.name: Spirit Guard.
entries: Kho adds 7 to his AC against one attack that would hit him, provided he can see the attacker.

legendary.name: Move.
entries: Kho moves up to his walk or climb speed.

legendary.name: Arm of Many.
entries: Kho makes an attack with his Arm of Many.

legendary.name: Fist (Costs 2 Actions).
entries: Kho makes a fist attack.

legendary.name: Manifest Missile (Costs 2 Actions).
entries: Kho uses Manifest Missile.

legendary.name: Show of Power (Costs 3 Actions).
entries: Kho uses one of his rechargeable abilities.

legendary.name: Cast a Spell (Costs 3 Actions).
entries: Kho casts a spell from his list of prepared spells, consuming a spell slot as normal.

legendaryHeader: Kho can take 3 legendary actions, choosing from the options below. Only one legendary action can be used at a time and only at the end of another creature's turn. Kho regains spent legendary actions at the start of his turn.

monster.name: Kho, Spiritual Form

trait.name: Spiritual Form.
entries: When Kho takes on his Spiritual Form, he retains his hit dice and hit point maximum from his previous form. Features with limited uses that are present across multiple forms, such as Legendary Resistance, do not regain spent uses. While in his Spiritual Form, Kho cannot take lair actions.

trait.name: Soul of Serenity.
entries: When Kho enters his spiritual form, his physical form melts away, dissolving into celestial light that scatters and dissipates. Kho teleports to an unoccupied space within 60 feet and uses Dreamwave. Kho can maintain this form for 1 minute, after which he returns to his physical form, loses his temporary hit points, and takes 5 points of exhaustion, which can only be removed by resting or a wish spell.

trait.name: Intangible.
entries: Kho is invisible, and he can occupy a creature's space and vice versa without treating it as difficult terrain. If any effect would cause Kho to become visible, he appears as a vaguely ape-shaped nebula of celestial light and stardust-like material.

trait.name: Magic Resistance.
entries: Kho has advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (3/day).
entries: If Kho fails a saving throw, he can choose to succeed instead.

action.name: Multiattack.
entries: Kho makes two Dreamtouch attacks and uses Induce Slumber or Tormented Awakening.

action.name: Dreamtouch.
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +14 to hit, reach 30 ft., one target. Hit: 13 (2d6 + 6) psychic damage, and for 1 minute, the target has disadvantage on Wisdom ability checks and saving throws.

action.name: Dreamwave (Recharge 5-6).
entries: Kho releases a wave of sleep-weave magics in a 30-foot radius centered on himself. All other creatures must make a DC 23 Wisdom saving throw or fall unconscious until the end of Kho's next turn on a failure.

action.name: Induce Slumber.
entries: Kho chooses one creature he can see within 120 feet. The creature must make a DC 23 Wisdom saving throw or fall unconscious for 1 minute and be subjected to a dream of Kho's choice. Up to six creatures can be under the effects of a dream at a time, and while at least one creature is dreaming, Kho is concentrating as if concentrating on a spell. Creatures who are unconscious but not yet in a dream automatically fail this saving throw.

items.name: 1. Dreams of Deep Waters
items.entry: While asleep, the creature dreams of deep, frozen waters. At the start of each of their turns, the creature must make a DC 23 Constitution saving throw, taking 22 (4d10) cold damage on a failure, or half as much on a success.

items.name: 2. Dreams of Spreading Fire
items.entry: While asleep, the creature dreams of engulfing flames. At the start of each of their turns, the creature must make a DC 23 Dexterity saving throw, taking 22 (4d10) fire damage on a failure, or half as much on a success.

items.name: 3. Dreams of Crushing Rock
items.entry: While asleep, the creature dreams of being crushed by falling rocks. The creature must make a DC 23 Strength saving throw, taking 18 (4d8) bludgeoning damage on a failure, or half as much on a success.

items.name: 4. Dreams of a Cage in the Darkness
items.entry: While asleep, the creature dreams of lightless imprisonment. The creature must make a DC 23 Intelligence saving throw, taking 18 (4d8) necrotic damage on a failure, or half as much on a success.

entries: If an unconscious creature succeeds on two of the saving throws against the dream, the creature wakes up, ending the dream. The dream also ends if the creature is reduced to 0 hit points or if Kho's concentration is broken.

action.name: Tormented Awakening.
entries: Kho chooses up to six sleeping creatures that he can see within 60 feet. Each creature wakes up and must make a DC 23 Wisdom saving throw, taking 36 (8d8) psychic damage on a failure, or half as much on a success.

legendary.name: Dreamtouch.
entries: Kho makes a Dreamtouch attack.

legendary.name: Dreamwalk.
entries: Kho moves up to half his fly speed.

legendary.name: Slumber (Costs 2 Actions).
entries: Kho uses Induce Slumber.

legendary.name: Awakening (Costs 2 Actions).
entries: Kho uses Tormented Awakening.

legendaryHeader: Kho can take 3 legendary actions, choosing from the options below. Only one legendary action option can be used at a time and only at the end of another creature's turn. Kho regains spent legendary actions at the start of his turn.

monster.name: Twr the Tyrant

trait.name: Spiritual Form.
entries: When Twr would be reduced to 0 hit points, she is instead reduced to 1 hit point. She gains a number of temporary hit points equal to half of her maximum hit points and ends all conditions affecting her. She then takes on her Spiritual Form, and her features and actions change accordingly.

trait.name: Fire Aura.
entries: At the start of each of Twr's turns, each creature within 5 feet of her takes 10 (3d6) fire damage, and flammable objects in the aura that aren't being worn or carried ignite. A creature that touches Twr or hits her with a melee attack while within 5 feet of her takes 10 (3d6) fire damage.

trait.name: Magic Resistance.
entries: Twr has advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

trait.name: Magic Weapons.
entries: Twr's weapon attacks are magical.

trait.name: Innate Spellcasting.
entries: Twr's spellcasting ability is Charisma (spell save DC 23). Twr can innately cast the following spells, requiring no material components:
At-will: blindness/deafness, command, cure wounds, healing word, hold person, inflict wounds, lesser restoration, pass without trace, scorching ray, shatter, silence, suggestion
6/day: banishment, bestow curse, blade barrier, contagion, control water, dimension door, dispel magic, harm, wall of fire
3/day: antimagic field, control weather, divine word, earthquake, firestorm, symbol
1/day: gate, imprisonment, meteor swarm

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (3/Day).
entries: If Twr fails a saving throw, she can choose to succeed instead.

action.name: Multiattack.
entries: Twr uses Scalding Spout and makes two attacks: one with her longsword and one with her whip. She can use Bolt of Flame in place of either or both of these attacks.

action.name: Longsword.
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +17 to hit, reach 15 ft., one target. Hit: 22 (3d8 + 9) slashing damage plus 13 (3d8) lightning damage.

action.name: Whip.
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +17 to hit, reach 30 ft., one target. Hit: 16 (2d6 + 9) slashing damage plus 7 (2d6) fire damage, and the target must succeed on a DC 24 Strength saving throw or be knocked prone and pulled up to 25 feet toward Twr.

action.name: Scalding Spout.
entries: Twr creates a spout of scalding water in a 10-foot-radius cylinder that is 30 feet high. All creatures in the area must make a DC 23 Dexterity saving throw, taking 35 (10d6) fire damage on a failure, or half as much on a success.

action.name: Bolt of Flame.
entries: Ranged Spell Attack: +15 to hit, range 120 ft., one target. Hit: 21 (4d6 + 7) fire damage.

action.name: Teleport.
entries: Twr magically teleports, along with any equipment she is wearing or carrying, up to 120 feet to an unoccupied space she can see.

action.name: Steam Breath (Recharge 5-6).
entries: Twr exhales scalding steam in a 60-foot cone. Each creature in the area must make a DC 23 Constitution saving throw, taking 70 (20d6) fire damage on a failure, or half as much on a success.

action.name: Whipcrack (Recharge 5-6).
entries: Twr cracks her whip, creating a wave of concussive sound. All creatures of Twr's choice within 60 feet of her must make a DC 23 Constitution saving throw, taking 22 (4d10) thunder damage and becoming stunned for 1 minute on a failure, or taking half as much damage on a success. A stunned creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of their turns, ending the effect on a success.

action.name: Gather Magic (2/Day).
entries: Twr focuses on her connection to the world's magic. If she has cast any spells with a per-day limit, the limit is reset as if she had not cast any such spells yet.

legendary.name: Fly.
entries: Twr moves up to half her fly speed.

legendary.name: Tyrant's Strike.
entries: Twr makes a longsword or whip attack.

legendary.name: Bolt of Flame.
entries: Twr uses Bolt of Flame.

legendary.name: Scalding Spout (Costs 2 Actions).
entries: Twr uses Scalding Spout.

legendary.name: Teleport (Costs 2 Actions).
entries: Twr uses Teleport.

legendary.name: Show of Power (Costs 3 Actions).
entries: Twr uses one of her rechargeable abilities, if available.

legendaryHeader: Twr can take 3 legendary actions, choosing from the options below. Only one legendary action option can be used at a time and only at the end of another creature's turn. Twr regains spent legendary actions at the start of her turn.

monster.name: Twr, Spiritual Form

trait.name: Spiritual Form.
entries: When Twr takes on her spiritual form, she retains her hit dice and hit point maximum from her previous form. Features with limited uses that are present across multiple forms, such as Legendary Resistance, do not regain spent uses. While in her Spiritual Form, Twr cannot take lair actions.

trait.name: Soul of Water and Flame.
entries: When Twr enters her spiritual form, her physical form is disintegrated in a burst of flame. All other creatures within 60 feet must make a DC 23 Dexterity saving throw, taking 70 (20d6) fire damage on a failure and falling prone on a failure, or taking half as much on a success. Twr's river hallow dries up as the water rises into Twr's spiritual form: a mass of roiling water that crudely imitates Twr's physical form. Twr can maintain this form for 1 minute, after which she returns to her physical form, loses her temporary hit points, and takes 5 points of exhaustion, which can only be removed by resting or a wish spell.

trait.name: Scalding Aura.
entries: Twr is heavily obscured by a veil of perpetual steam, and when a creature starts their turn within 15 feet of her, they take 14 (4d6) fire damage. A creature that touches Twr or hits her with a melee attack while within 5 feet of her takes 14 (4d6) fire damage.

trait.name: Magic Resistance.
entries: Twr has advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

trait.name: Magic Weapons.
entries: Twr's weapon attacks are magical.

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (3/Day).
entries: If Twr fails a saving throw, she can choose to succeed instead.

action.name: Multiattack.
entries: Twr makes two furylash attacks and uses either Grasping Current or Essence Bolt.

action.name: Furylash.
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +17 to hit, reach 30 ft., one target. Hit: 19 (4d6 + 9) bludgeoning damage plus 14 (4d6) fire damage.

action.name: Essence Bolt.
entries: Ranged Spell Attack: +15 to hit, range 120 ft., one target. Hit: the target is subject to one of the following effects:

items.name: 1. Essence of Fire
items.entry: The target takes 33 (6d10) fire damage and must make a DC 23 Dexterity saving throw or ignite and burn for 1 minute. At the end of a burning creature's turn, they can repeat the Dexterity saving throw, taking 16 (3d10) fire damage on a failure and ending the burning effect on a success.

items.name: 2. Essence of Water
items.entry: The target takes 33 (6d10) bludgeoning damage and must make a DC 23 Strength saving throw or be restrained by watery tethers. At the end of a restrained creature's turn, they can repeat the Strength saving throw, ending the restrained effect on a success.

action.name: Grasping Current.
entries: Twr chooses a creature within 60 feet. That creature must make a DC 23 Strength saving throw, taking 21 (6d6) bludgeoning damage and being moved up to 30 feet in a direction Twr chooses on a failure, or taking half as much damage and not being moved on a success.

action.name: Teleport.
entries: Twr magically teleports, along with any equipment it is wearing or carrying, up to 120 feet to an unoccupied space she can see.

action.name: Wrath of the Boiling One (Recharge 5-6).
entries: Scalding water erupts in five 10-foot-radius cylinders that are 60 feet high at points of Twr's choosing within 120 feet. Each creature in a cylinder's area must make a DC 23 Dexterity saving throw, taking 66 (12d10) fire damage on a failure, or half as much on a success.

legendary.name: Fly.
entries: Twr moves up to half her fly speed.

legendary.name: Flamelash.
entries: Twr makes a Furylash attack.

legendary.name: Unleashed Power (Costs 2 Actions).
entries: Twr uses either Essence Bolt or Grasping Current

legendary.name: Teleport (Costs 2 Actions).
entries: Twr uses Teleport.

legendary.name: Wrath (Costs 3 Actions).
entries: Twr uses Wrath of the Boiling One, if available.

legendaryHeader: Twr can take 3 legendary actions, choosing from the options below. Only one legendary action option can be used at a time and only at the end of another creature's turn. Twr regains spent legendary actions at the start of her turn.

monster.name: Unkillable Urhosh

trait.name: Spiritual Form.
entries: When Urhosh would be reduced to 0 hit points, he is instead reduced to 1 hit point. He gains a number of temporary hit points equal to half of his maximum hit points and ends all conditions affecting him. He then takes on his Spiritual Form, and his features and actions change accordingly.

trait.name: Regeneration.
entries: Urhosh regains 30 hit points at the start of his turn if he has at least 1 hit point. If Urhosh takes 30 or more necrotic damage in a single turn, this trait doesn't function at the start of his next turn.

trait.name: Spirit Hive.
entries: Urhosh is surrounded by a swarm of spirits that take the form of buzzing bees. The hive extends 10 feet from Urhosh in every direction. When a creature starts their turn within the hive or enters it for the first time on a turn, they must make a DC 21 Constitution saving throw, taking 13 (3d8) radiant damage on a failure, or half as much on a success. Urhosh can choose any number of creatures to be immune to this effect.

trait.name: Magic Resistance.
entries: Urhosh has advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

trait.name: Magic Weapons.
entries: Urhosh's weapon attacks are magical.

trait.name: Innate Spellcasting.
entries: Urhosh's spellcasting ability is Charisma (spell save DC 21). Urhosh can innately cast the following spells, requiring no material components:
At-will: aid, command, cure wounds, detect evil and good, detect magic, guidance, guiding bolt, healing word, hold person, lesser restoration, protection from poison, sacred flame
6/day: banishment, confusion, create food and water, dispel evil and good, greater restoration, hallow, heal, heroes' feast, insect plague
3/day: divine word, earthquake, holy aura, planeshift, regenerate, symbol
1/day: astral projection, mass heal, gate

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (3/Day).
entries: If Urhosh fails a saving throw, he can choose to succeed instead.

action.name: Multiattack.
entries: Urhosh makes three attacks: two with his claws or Ferocious Swipe and one with his bite. He can replace any of these attacks with Conjure Weapon.

action.name: Claw.
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +15 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 18 (3d6 + 8) slashing damage, or 29 (6d6 + 8) slashing damage if the target is prone.

action.name: Bite.
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +15 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 26 (4d8 + 8) piercing damage. The target must make a DC 23 Strength saving throw or be knocked prone.

action.name: Conjure Weapon.
entries: Ranged Spell Attack: +13 to hit, range 120 ft., one target. Hit: 17 (2d10 + 6) radiant damage.

action.name: Ferocious Swipe (Recharge 4-6).
entries: Urhosh makes a claw attack with advantage against a creature within reach. On a hit, the creature suffers the attack's effects as normal, and a Large or smaller creature must make a DC 23 Strength saving throw or be hurled up to 60 feet in a direction of Urhosh's choice. If it lands or collides with a surface that halts its movement, the creature takes 1d6 bludgeoning damage for every 10 feet traveled and falls prone.

action.name: Daunting Roar (Recharge 5-6).
entries: Urhosh unleashes a bone-shaking roar. All creatures within 90 feet that can hear him must make a DC 21 Wisdom saving throw. A creature that fails its saving throw becomes frightened for 1 minute, and must use its action to move as far as its speed allows away from Urhosh. A frightened creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of their turns, ending the effect on a success.

action.name: Gregarious Word (Recharge 5-6).
entries: Urhosh makes a good-natured remark laced with enchantment magic. All other creatures within 60 feet of Urhosh that can hear him must make a DC 21 Wisdom saving throw or become charmed by Urhosh for 1 minute. Whenever the charmed creature takes damage, it can repeat the saving throw, ending the charmed effect on a success.

action.name: Gather Magic (2/Day).
entries: Urhosh focuses on his connection to the world's magic. If he has cast any spells with a per-day limit, the limit is reset as if he had not cast any such spells yet.

legendary.name: Move.
entries: Urhosh moves up to half his speed.

legendary.name: Claw.
entries: Urhosh makes a claw attack.

legendary.name: Conjure Weapon.
entries: Urhosh uses Conjure Weapon.

legendary.name: Incite Laughter.
entries: Urhosh casts hideous laughter without expending a spell slot.

legendary.name: Regenerate (Costs 2 Actions).
entries: Urhosh heals himself for 30 hit points. Urhosh cannot take this legendary action if his Regeneration feature isn't functioning.

legendary.name: Show of Power (Costs 3 Actions).
entries: Urhosh uses one of his rechargeable abilities, if available.

legendaryHeader: Urhosh can take 3 legendary actions, choosing from the options below. Only one legendary action option can be used at a time and only at the end of another creature's turn. Urhosh regains spent legendary actions at the start of his turn.

monster.name: Urhosh, Spiritual Form

trait.name: Spiritual Form.
entries: When Urhosh takes on his spiritual form, he retains his hit dice and hit point maximum from his previous form. Features with limited uses that are present across multiple forms, such as Legendary Resistance, do not regain spent uses. While in his Spiritual Form, Urhosh cannot take lair actions.

trait.name: Unbound Life.
entries: When Urhosh's spiritual form triggers, his body becomes wreathed in crackling life energy, and the plant life in his hollow undergoes explosive growth. The ground in Urhosh's hollow becomes difficult terrain for all other creatures, and all creatures of Urhosh's choice must make a DC 25 Strength saving throw or be restrained by grasping vines, provided they are on the ground. A restrained creature can be freed if it or another creature takes an action to make a DC 21 Strength check and succeeds. The vines can also be attacked and destroyed (AC 15; hp 25; vulnerability to slashing damage; immunity to psychic damage). Urhosh can maintain this form for 1 minute, after which he returns to his physical form, loses his temporary hit points, and is affected as by 5 points of exhaustion, which can only be removed by resting or a wish spell.

trait.name: Awakened Hive.
entries: Urhosh is surrounded by a swarm of spirits that take the form of buzzing bees. The hive extends 15 feet from Urhosh in every direction, and when a creature starts their turn within the hive's area or enters it for the first time on a turn, they must make a DC 25 Constitution saving throw, taking 18 (4d8) radiant damage on a failure, or half as much on a success. Urhosh can choose any number of creatures to be immune to this effect. Additionally, when a creature within the hive's area receives magical healing, the healing they receive is reduced to 0 and they take 18 (4d8) radiant damage.

trait.name: Magic Resistance.
entries: Urhosh has advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

trait.name: Magic Weapons.
entries: Urhosh's weapon attacks are magical.

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (3/Day).
entries: If Urhosh fails a saving throw, he can choose to succeed instead.

action.name: Multiattack.
entries: Urhosh uses Overflow or Absorb Life. Then he makes two claws attacks and one bite attack. He can replace any of these three attacks with Conjure Weapon.

action.name: Claw.
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +15 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 18 (3d6 + 8) slashing damage, or 29 (6d6 + 8) slashing damage if the target is prone.

action.name: Bite.
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +15 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 34 (4d12 + 8), and the target must make a DC 23 Strength saving throw or fall prone.

action.name: Conjure Weapon.
entries: Ranged Spell Attack: +13 to hit, range 120 ft., one target. Hit: 17 (2d10 + 6) radiant damage.

action.name: Overflow.
entries: Urhosh chooses a creature within 60 feet that he can see, and attempts to oversaturate it with life energy. The creature must make a DC 25 Constitution saving throw, taking 33 (6d10) radiant damage on a failure, or half as much on a success. A creature that fails their saving throw by 5 or more is incapacitated until the end of their next turn.

action.name: Absorb Life.
entries: Urhosh chooses a creature within 5 feet that he can see and attempts to absorb its life force into his own. The creature must make a DC 25 Constitution saving throw, taking 44 (8d10) necrotic damage on a failure. If this damage reduces the creature to 0 hit points, it is absorbed, leaving behind any equipment and armor it was wearing. The absorbed creature can be restored to life by nothing short of a wish spell.

action.name: Life's Weight (Recharge 5-6).
entries: Urhosh creates a magical field of crushing weight in a 20-foot radius, 80-foot-high cylinder centered on a point that Urhosh can see within 120 feet. All creatures in the area must make a DC 25 Strength saving throw, taking 27 (5d10) bludgeoning damage and 27 (5d10) radiant damage and falling prone on a failure, or taking half as much damage on a success.

legendary.name: Move.
entries: Urhosh moves up to his speed.

legendary.name: Claw.
entries: Urhosh makes a claw attack.

legendary.name: Conjure Weapon.
entries: Urhosh uses conjure weapon.

legendary.name: Bite (Costs 2 Actions).
entries: Urhosh makes a bite attack.

legendary.name: Unleashed Power (Costs 2 Actions).
entries: Urhosh uses overflow or absorb life.

legendary.name: Life's Weight (Costs 3 Actions).
entries: Urhosh uses life's weight, if available.

legendaryHeader: Urhosh can take 3 legendary actions, choosing from the options below. Only one legendary action option can be used at a time and only at the end of another creature's turn. Urhosh regains spent legendary actions at the start of his turn.

trait.name: Charge.
entries: If the arctusk moves at least 20 feet straight toward a creature and then hits it with a bash attack on the same turn, that creature must succeed on a DC 13 Strength saving throw or be knocked prone. If the target is prone, the arctusk can make one tusk attack against the target.

trait.name: Musk Madness.
entries: Male arctusks in mating season emit musk that can be smelled in a 1-mile radius. A musking arctusk uses its action to attack a randomly determined creature within its reach, unless that creature is a female arctusk, and has advantage on melee weapon attacks. Attack rolls against it have advantage.

action.name: Bash.
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +7 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 18 (3d8 + 5) bludgeoning damage.

action.name: Tusk.
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +7 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 21 (3d10 + 5) piercing damage.

trait.name: Pack Tactics.
entries: The batface has advantage on attack rolls against a creature if at least one of the batface's allies is within 5 feet of the creature and the ally isn't incapacitated.

action.name: Bind.
entries: As an action, the batface can make a Dexterity (Stealth) check versus the passive Perception of a Large or smaller sleeping creature to secure it with ropes and nets. On a success, the sleeping creature is restrained.

The bindings have AC 10 and 10 hit points. A creature can use its action to make a DC 10 Strength check to free itself or another creature within reach. Each time a batface uses the bind action on the same creature, the DC to free that creature increases by 1.

action.name: Bite.
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +3 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 1d4+3 piercing damage, and the target must make a DC 12 Constitution saving throw against batface toxin. On a failure, the target is charmed by the toxin's euphoric effect and regards all creatures as friendly for up to 1 hour.

At the end of its next turn, the target can repeat the saving throw, ending the effect on a success. On a failure, if the creature has less than half of its hit point maximum, it falls unconscious for 1 hour or until woken by magic such as protection from poison or lesser restoration.

trait.name: Rumbling Trample.
entries: Once per turn, immediately after the bouldertail moves at least 15 feet (up to a maximum of 30 feet), all creatures that the bouldertail passed within 10 feet of must succeed on a DC 16 Strength or Dexterity saving throw or take 19 (4d6+5) bludgeoning damage. A Huge or smaller creature that fails its saving throw is also knocked prone.

action.name: Multiattack.
entries: The bouldertail uses Body Slam and makes a Tail attack.

action.name: Body Slam.
entries: The bouldertail throws its weight against a creature it can see within 5 feet. That creature must succeed on a DC 16 Strength saving throw, taking 13 (2d6 + 5) bludgeoning damage on a failure, or half as much on a success. A Large or smaller creature that fails its saving throw is pushed up to 10 feet away, where it falls prone.

action.name: Tail.
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +8 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 15 (2d8 + 5) bludgeoning damage, and the bouldertail subjects the target to one of the following effects:

items: Launch. A target that is Large or smaller and is not prone, must succeed on a DC 16 Strength saving throw or be launched up to 15 feet away, where it lands prone, taking 3 (1d6) bludgeoning damage from the fall.
Crush. If the target is prone, it takes an additional 9 (2d8) bludgeoning damage.

bonus.name: Groundpound.
entries: The bouldertail makes a tail attack against a prone creature.

trait.name: Skilled Mimic.
entries: The chattercrest can imitate complex sounds and speech patterns it's heard before up to 1 minute in length. A creature that hears the sounds can tell they are imitations with a successful DC 8 Wisdom (Insight) check.

action.name: Bite.
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 1 piercing damage.

trait.name: Apex Ambusher.
entries: In the first round of a combat, the doomclaw has advantage on attack rolls against any creature it surprised. Any hit the doomclaw scores against a surprised creature is a critical hit.

trait.name: Keen Sight and Smell.
entries: The doomclaw has advantage on Wisdom (Perception) checks that rely on sight or smell.

trait.name: Natural Camouflage.
entries: The doomclaw has advantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks made to hide.

trait.name: Pack Tactics.
entries: The doomclaw has advantage on attack rolls against a creature if at least one of the doomclaw's allies is within 5 feet of the creature and the ally isn't incapacitated.

trait.name: Pounce.
entries: If the doomclaw moves at least 20 feet straight toward a creature and then hits it with a claw attack on the same turn, that target must succeed on a DC 15 Strength saving throw or take an additional 7 (2d6) slashing damage and fall prone.

action.name: Multiattack.
entries: The doomclaw makes two attacks: one with its bite and one with its claws.

action.name: Bite.
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +7 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 15 (4d4 + 5) piercing damage, and if the target is Large or smaller, it is grappled (escape DC 14).

action.name: Claws.
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +7 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 13 (2d6 + 5) slashing damage.

reaction.name: Opportunistic Strike.
entries: When a creature within 5 feet of the doomclaw makes an attack against a target other than the doomclaw, the doomclaw can make an opportunity attack against the attacking creature.

trait.name: Pack Tactics.
entries: The farloper has advantage on attack rolls against a creature if at least one of the farloper's allies is within 5 feet of the creature and the ally isn't incapacitated.

trait.name: Sprinter.
entries: The farloper can Dash as a bonus action.

action.name: Kick.
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +6 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 11 (2d6 + 4) slashing damage.

trait.name: Keen Smell.
entries: The flintback has advantage on Wisdom (Perception) checks that rely on smell.

trait.name: Ignite Scales.
entries: At will, a flintback can ignite the plate-like scales on its back. These cast bright light in a 30-foot radius and dim light for an additional 30 feet.

trait.name: Shimmering Scales.
entries: At the start of each of its turns while it isn't incapacitated, the flintback shifts its plate-like scales, creating a shimmering pattern of color in two 15-foot cubes originating from the flintback, which are positioned opposite each other. Any creature in a cube's area who can see the flintback must succeed on a DC 14 Wisdom saving throw or become charmed until the end of its next turn. While charmed in this way, a creature is incapacitated and has a speed of 0.
The charm ends for an affected creature if it takes any damage or if someone else uses an action to shake the creature out of its stupor.

action.name: Multiattack.
entries: The flintback makes two tail attacks.

action.name: Tail.
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +9 to hit, reach 10 ft. Hit: 16 (3d6 + 6) piercing damage plus 7 (2d6) bludgeoning damage.

action.name: Sweeping Tail.
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +9 to hit, reach 10 ft., two targets. Hit: 13 (2d6 + 6) piercing damage plus 7 (2d6) bludgeoning damage, and the target must succeed on a DC 17 Strength saving throw or be knocked prone.

reaction.name: Parry.
entries: The flintback adds +3 to its AC against one melee attack that would hit it. To do so, the flintback must see the attacker.

trait.name: Startling Display.
entries: As a bonus action, the frilled spitter can expand its frill, displaying its bright colors with a sudden show. When it uses this feature, creatures that can see it cannot take attacks of opportunity against the frilled spitter for the rest of its turn. Creatures cannot be affected by the same frilled spitter's Startling Display more than once.

action.name: Bite.
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 6 (1d6 + 3) piercing damage plus 3 (1d6) acid damage.

action.name: Acid Spit (Recharge 5–6).
entries: The frilled spitter spews acid at a creature it can see within 30 feet. The target must make a DC 13 Dexterity saving throw. On a failure, it takes 7 (2d6) acid damage and is blinded for 1 minute. On a success, it takes half damage and is not blinded. A creature blinded by the acid spit can repeat its saving throw at the start of its turn, ending the effect on a success.

action.name: Multiattack.
entries: The great-hook makes three attacks: two with its claws and one stomp attack.

action.name: Claws.
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +9 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 19 (3d6 + 6) slashing damage. If the target is a Large or smaller creature, it must make a DC 17 Strength saving throw, falling prone on a failure.

action.name: Stomp.
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +9 to hit, reach 5 ft., one prone target. Hit: 27 (6d6 +6) bludgeoning damage.

monster.name: Deathwalker

trait.name: Keen Sight and Smell.
entries: The deathwalker has advantage on Wisdom (Perception) checks that rely on sight or smell.

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (3/day).
entries: If the deathwalker fails a saving throw, it can choose to succeed instead.

trait.name: Strength Sense.
entries: The deathwalker is aware of the presence of creatures within 5 miles of it that have a Constitution or Strength score of 16 or higher. It knows the approximate distance and direction to each creature, as well as each creature's Constitution and Strength scores.

trait.name: Titanic Impact.
entries: Once on each of its turns, immediately after the deathwalker moves at least 20 feet straight towards a creature on the ground and comes within 5 feet of it, the deathwalker can opt to end its movement in a titanic impact. All other creatures within 10 feet of the deathwalker when it ends its movement in this way must succeed on a DC 25 Strength or Dexterity saving throw, taking 36 (8d6 + 9) bludgeoning damage on a failure. A huge or smaller creature that fails its saving throw is pushed up to 20 feet away, where it falls prone.

trait.name: Tyrant's Hide.
entries: Whenever the deathwalker takes bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing damage, it is reduced by 7.

trait.name: Withstand (3/day).
entries: If the deathwalker takes damage from an attack that was not a critical hit, it can reduce the damage from the attack to 0.

action.name: Multiattack.
entries: The deathwalker makes two attacks: one with its bite or slam and one with its tail.

action.name: Bite.
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +17 to hit, reach 15 ft., one target. Hit: 48 (6d12 + 9) piercing damage. If the target is a Large or smaller creature, it is grappled (escape DC 20). Until this grapple ends, the target is restrained, and the deathwalker can't bite another target. If the deathwalker scores a critical hit, it rolls damage dice three times, instead of twice.

action.name: Slam.
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +17 to hit, reach 15 ft., one target. Hit: 25 (4d6 + 9) bludgeoning damage. If the target is a Large or smaller creature, it is hurled up to 30 feet in a direction of the deathwalker's choice and lands prone, taking 10 (3d6) bludgeoning damage from the fall.

action.name: Tail.
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +17 to hit, reach 20 ft., two targets that are not grappled by the deathwalker. Hit: 27 (4d8 + 9) bludgeoning damage, and the target must succeed on a DC 25 Strength saving throw or be knocked prone.

action.name: Fearsome Roar (Recharge 5-6).
